# WSO2 API Manager Documentation

WSO2 API Manager is a comprehensive, fully open-source API management platform that provides complete API lifecycle management with enterprise-grade security, advanced gateway capabilities, and AI-powered features. It enables organizations to design, secure, publish, and analyze APIs while providing developers with a rich marketplace experience. Some of its features include:
- An API Publisher for creating and managing APIs
- Developer Portal for discovery and subscription
- Classic Gateway for runtime and backend component (an API proxy) for API calls.
- Platform Gateway for centrally managed hybrid deployments 
- AI Gateway for governing LLM and MCP traffic. 

WSO2 API Manager supports API types such as REST, GraphQL, WebSocket, WebSub/WebHook, SSE, and AI. It can be deployed on VMs, Kubernetes (EKS, AKS, GKE, OpenShift), or in multi-datacenter configurations.

## Get Started

- [Home](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/index.md): Landing page for WSO2 API Manager documentation
- [Introduction](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/get-started/overview.md): Overview of WSO2 API Manager — core capabilities including API design, security, gateway, AI APIs, and the portals that make up the platform
- [Key Concepts](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/get-started/key-concepts.md): Glossary of foundational API Manager concepts — API, Application, Subscription, Rate Limiting, Lifecycle, Tenant, Service Catalog, and user roles
- [Quick Start Guide](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/get-started/api-manager-quick-start-guide.md): Step-by-step guide to create, publish, and invoke your first API using the Publisher and Developer Portals, runnable on VM or Docker
- [Architecture](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/get-started/apim-architecture.md): High-level architecture of WSO2 API Manager — API Control Plane, Classic Gateway, Platform Gateway, Traffic Manager, and Key Manager and how they interact
- [Deployment Platforms](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/get-started/deployment-platforms.md): Comparison of deployment platform choices — Virtual Machines vs. Kubernetes — and guidance on selecting the right one
- [Deployment Patterns](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/get-started/deployment-patterns.md): Overview of the three main deployment pattern categories — All-in-One, Distributed, and Multi-Datacenter — and when to use each
- [About this Release](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/get-started/about-this-release.md): What is new in WSO2 API Manager 4.7.0 — new features, improvements, deprecated features, and download links

## Install & Setup

- [Install and Setup Overview](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/install-and-setup/install-and-setup-overview.md): Overview of the installation and setup process — installation, deployment, and configuration topics organized by component
- [Installation Prerequisites](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/install-and-setup/install/installation-prerequisites.md): OS, JDK, and database compatibility requirements before installing any API Manager runtime
- [Install API Manager Runtime](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/install-and-setup/install/installing-the-product/installing-api-m-runtime.md): Step-by-step instructions to download and install the WSO2 API Manager runtime on a VM
- [Run API Manager](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/install-and-setup/install/installing-the-product/running-the-api-m.md): How to start the API Manager server and access the Publisher, Developer Portal, and Management Console web UIs
- [Run API Manager as a Linux Service](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/install-and-setup/install/installing-the-product/installing-api-m-as-a-linux-service.md): Configure WSO2 API Manager to run as a background Linux service using the System V init (`update-rc.d`) mechanism
- [Run API Manager as a Windows Service](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/install-and-setup/install/installing-the-product/installing-api-m-as-a-windows-service.md): Configure WSO2 API Manager to run as a Windows Service using the YAJSW wrapper
- [Installation Options](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/install-and-setup/install/installation-options.md): Alternative installation methods — Kubernetes Helm charts, Docker/Docker Compose, and Puppet
- [Update WSO2 API Manager](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/install-and-setup/setup/updating-wso2-api-manager.md): Apply WSO2 Updates (bug fixes and security patches) to an existing API Manager installation using the WSO2 Updates 2.0 tool
- [Set Up Kubernetes Gateway with APIM](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/install-and-setup/setup/distributed-deployment/configuring-apim-as-a-gateway.md): Configure the WSO2 API Control Plane to work with the Kubernetes-native Platform Gateway as the data plane
- [Set Up a Third-party Key Manager](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/install-and-setup/setup/distributed-deployment/configure-a-third-party-key-manager.md): Configure an external OAuth2 key manager (e.g., WSO2 IS, Keycloak) to replace the built-in Key Manager component
- [Set Up WSO2 Identity Server as a Key Manager](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/install-and-setup/setup/distributed-deployment/configuring-wso2-identity-server-as-a-key-manager.md): Configure WSO2 Identity Server as the resident Key Manager for API authentication and token issuance
- [Database Setup Overview](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/install-and-setup/setup/setting-up-databases/overview.md): Overview of the API Manager databases (AM DB, Shared DB) and guidance on matching database setup to your deployment pattern
- [Change to MySQL](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/install-and-setup/setup/setting-up-databases/changing-default-databases/changing-to-mysql.md): Replace the default H2 database with MySQL for the API Manager and Shared databases
- [Change to MSSQL](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/install-and-setup/setup/setting-up-databases/changing-default-databases/changing-to-mssql.md): Replace the default H2 database with Microsoft SQL Server
- [Change to PostgreSQL](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/install-and-setup/setup/setting-up-databases/changing-default-databases/changing-to-postgresql.md): Replace the default H2 database with PostgreSQL
- [Change to Oracle](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/install-and-setup/setup/setting-up-databases/changing-default-databases/changing-to-oracle.md): Replace the default H2 database with Oracle Database
- [Change to IBM DB2](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/install-and-setup/setup/setting-up-databases/changing-default-databases/changing-to-ibm-db2.md): Replace the default H2 database with IBM DB2
- [Change to Oracle RAC](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/install-and-setup/setup/setting-up-databases/changing-default-databases/changing-to-oracle-rac.md): Replace the default H2 database with Oracle Real Application Clusters (RAC)
- [Manage Data Growth and Improve Performance](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/install-and-setup/setup/setting-up-databases/managing-data-growth-and-improving-performance.md): Strategies for managing database growth, archiving old data, and improving query performance in production
- [Configure Proxy Server and Load Balancer](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/install-and-setup/setup/setting-up-proxy-server-and-the-load-balancer/configuring-the-proxy-server-and-the-load-balancer.md): Configure a reverse proxy or load balancer in front of WSO2 API Manager
- [Add a Custom Proxy Path](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/install-and-setup/setup/setting-up-proxy-server-and-the-load-balancer/adding-a-custom-proxy-path.md): Serve API Manager components under a custom URL context path via a proxy
- [Symmetric Encryption](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/install-and-setup/setup/security/encryption/symmetric-encryption.md): How API Manager uses symmetric encryption (default from 4.x) to protect sensitive data at rest, and how to configure the secret key
- [Asymmetric Encryption](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/install-and-setup/setup/security/encryption/asymmetric-encryption.md): How to switch from symmetric to asymmetric (public/private key) encryption for sensitive data
- [Maintain Logins and Passwords](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/install-and-setup/setup/security/logins-and-passwords/maintaining-logins-and-passwords.md): Overview of login and password management options in WSO2 API Manager
- [Customize Secure Vault](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/install-and-setup/setup/security/logins-and-passwords/carbon-secure-vault-implementation.md): Use the Carbon Secure Vault to protect plain-text passwords in configuration files
- [Set Passwords Using Environment Variables](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/install-and-setup/setup/security/logins-and-passwords/set-passwords-using-vars-and-sys-props.md): Supply configuration passwords via environment variables or Java system properties instead of plain text
- [Work with Encrypted Passwords](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/install-and-setup/setup/security/logins-and-passwords/working-with-encrypted-passwords.md): Encrypt plain-text passwords in configuration files using the Cipher Tool
- [Set Up ReCaptcha](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/install-and-setup/setup/security/logins-and-passwords/setting-up-recaptcha.md): Enable Google reCaptcha on Developer Portal login, self-registration, and password recovery forms
- [Configure reCaptcha for Single Sign-On](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/install-and-setup/setup/security/logins-and-passwords/configuring-recaptcha-for-single-sign-on.md): Apply reCaptcha challenges to SSO login flows in WSO2 API Manager
- [Integrate with HashiCorp Vault](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/install-and-setup/setup/security/logins-and-passwords/harshicrop-vault-extension.md): Use HashiCorp Vault as an external secret store for API Manager configuration passwords
- [Configure Keystores in API Manager](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/install-and-setup/setup/security/configuring-keystores/configuring-keystores-in-wso2-api-manager.md): Configure TLS keystores and truststores for WSO2 API Manager components
- [About Keystores and Truststores](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/install-and-setup/setup/security/configuring-keystores/keystore-basics/about-keystores-and-truststores.md): Concepts and terminology around keystores, truststores, and digital certificates in WSO2 products
- [Create a New Keystore](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/install-and-setup/setup/security/configuring-keystores/keystore-basics/creating-new-keystores.md): Generate a new keystore and self-signed certificate using the Java keytool
- [Renew a CA-Signed Certificate](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/install-and-setup/setup/security/configuring-keystores/keystore-basics/renewing-a-ca-signed-certificate-in-a-keystore.md): Replace an expiring CA-signed certificate in an existing keystore without recreating it
- [Enable Hostname Verification](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/install-and-setup/setup/security/enabling-hostname-verification.md): Enable TLS hostname verification for outbound connections from the API Manager runtime
- [GDPR for WSO2 API Manager](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/install-and-setup/setup/security/general-data-protection-regulation-gdpr-for-wso2-api-manager.md): GDPR compliance features in WSO2 API Manager — personal data handling, the Forget-Me tool, and data purging
- [Configure Transport Level Security](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/install-and-setup/setup/security/configuring-transport-level-security.md): Configure TLS/SSL protocols and cipher suites for API Manager HTTP transports
- [User Account Management](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/install-and-setup/setup/security/user-account-management.md): Configure account locking, password policies, and login attempt limits
- [Secure Web Portals](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/install-and-setup/setup/security/securing-api-m-web-portals.md): Security hardening for the Publisher and Developer Portal web applications
- [Introduction to User Stores](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/install-and-setup/setup/configure-userstores/introduction-to-userstores.md): Overview of user store types in WSO2 API Manager — JDBC, LDAP, and Active Directory
- [Configure the Primary User Store](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/install-and-setup/setup/configure-userstores/configure-primary-user-store/configuring-the-primary-user-store.md): Configure the primary user store that API Manager uses for authentication and user management
- [Configure a JDBC User Store](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/install-and-setup/setup/configure-userstores/configure-primary-user-store/configuring-a-jdbc-user-store.md): Use a relational database as the primary user store
- [Configure a Read-Write LDAP User Store](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/install-and-setup/setup/configure-userstores/configure-primary-user-store/configuring-a-read-write-ldap-user-store.md): Connect WSO2 API Manager to an LDAP directory for read-write user management
- [Configure a Read-Only LDAP User Store](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/install-and-setup/setup/configure-userstores/configure-primary-user-store/configuring-a-read-only-ldap-user-store.md): Connect WSO2 API Manager to an LDAP directory in read-only mode
- [Configure a Read-Write Active Directory User Store](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/install-and-setup/setup/configure-userstores/configure-primary-user-store/configuring-a-read-write-active-directory-user-store.md): Connect WSO2 API Manager to Microsoft Active Directory for user management
- [Configure Identity Server as External IDP (OIDC)](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/install-and-setup/setup/sso/configuring-identity-server-as-external-idp-using-oidc.md): Set up WSO2 Identity Server as an external identity provider for API Manager portals using OIDC
- [Configure Identity Server as External IDP (SAML)](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/install-and-setup/setup/sso/configuring-identity-server-as-external-idp-using-saml.md): Set up WSO2 Identity Server as an external identity provider for API Manager portals using SAML2
- [OKTA as External IDP (OIDC)](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/install-and-setup/setup/sso/okta-as-an-external-idp-using-oidc.md): Configure Okta as an external identity provider for API Manager SSO using OpenID Connect
- [OKTA as External IDP (SAML)](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/install-and-setup/setup/sso/okta-as-an-external-idp-using-saml.md): Configure Okta as an external identity provider for API Manager SSO using SAML2
- [Change the Default Transport](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/install-and-setup/setup/advance-configurations/changing-the-default-transport.md): Configure the HTTP PassThrough transport parameters (receiver and sender) used by the API Manager Gateway
- [Configure Caching](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/install-and-setup/setup/advance-configurations/configuring-caching.md): Configure key validation, resource, JWT, and response caching in the API Gateway
- [Customize the Management Console](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/install-and-setup/setup/advance-configurations/customizing-the-management-console.md): Customize the look and feel of the Carbon Management Console
- [Configure the Crypto Provider](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/install-and-setup/setup/advance-configurations/configuring-the-crypto-provider.md): Switch the cryptographic provider used by WSO2 API Manager (e.g., Bouncy Castle FIPS)
- [Pattern 0 - All-in-One Setup](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/install-and-setup/setup/single-node/configuring-a-single-node.md): Configure and deploy WSO2 API Manager as a single standalone node with all components on one JVM
- [Pattern 1 - All-in-One HA Setup](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/install-and-setup/setup/single-node/configuring-an-active-active-deployment.md): Configure an active-active high availability deployment with two All-in-One API Manager nodes behind a load balancer
- [Pattern 2 - Simple Scalable Setup](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/install-and-setup/setup/distributed-deployment/deploying-wso2-api-m-in-a-simple-scalable-setup.md): Deploy API Manager in a distributed setup with the API Control Plane and Classic Gateway as separate nodes
- [Pattern 3 - Distributed Setup](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/install-and-setup/setup/distributed-deployment/deploying-wso2-api-m-in-a-distributed-setup.md): Deploy API Manager with the API Control Plane, Traffic Manager, and Classic Gateway as fully separate distributed nodes
- [Pattern 4 - Distributed Setup with Key Manager Separation](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/install-and-setup/setup/distributed-deployment/deploying-wso2-api-m-in-a-distributed-setup-with-km-separated.md): Fully distributed deployment with a dedicated external Key Manager node (e.g., WSO2 IS)
- [Multi-DC Deployment Patterns Overview](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/install-and-setup/setup/multi-dc-deployment/multi-dc-deployment-patterns-overview.md): Overview of multi-datacenter deployment architectures for API Manager — use cases, patterns, and design considerations
- [Multi-DC Deployment - Pattern 1](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/install-and-setup/setup/multi-dc-deployment/configuring-multi-dc-deployment-pattern-1.md): Geo-regional synchronized deployment where all regions are identical and share replicated databases — configure a single region and replicate across DCs
- [Multi-DC Deployment - Pattern 2](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/install-and-setup/setup/multi-dc-deployment/configuring-multi-dc-deployment-pattern-2.md): Centralized API management with regional data planes — one main region hosts the Control Plane and sub-regions run Gateway and Traffic Manager nodes that connect back to it
- [Kubernetes Overview](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/install-and-setup/setup/kubernetes-deployment/kubernetes/kubernetes-overview.md): Overview of deploying WSO2 API Manager on Kubernetes using Helm charts, available patterns, and when to use each
- [Kubernetes Quick Start](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/install-and-setup/setup/kubernetes-deployment/kubernetes/kubernetes-quick-start.md): Get WSO2 API Manager running on Kubernetes quickly using Helm with minimal configuration
- [K8s Pattern 0 - All-in-One Setup](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/install-and-setup/setup/kubernetes-deployment/kubernetes/am-pattern-0-all-in-one.md): Deploy all API Manager components in a single pod on Kubernetes for evaluation or development
- [K8s Pattern 1 - All-in-One HA Setup](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/install-and-setup/setup/kubernetes-deployment/kubernetes/am-pattern-1-all-in-one-ha.md): Deploy an active-active, high-availability All-in-One API Manager setup on Kubernetes
- [K8s Pattern 2 - Simple Scalable Setup](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/install-and-setup/setup/kubernetes-deployment/kubernetes/am-pattern-2-all-in-one-gw.md): Deploy a dedicated Classic Gateway alongside the All-in-One node so the Gateway can scale independently from the Control Plane
- [K8s Pattern 3 - Distributed Setup](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/install-and-setup/setup/kubernetes-deployment/kubernetes/am-pattern-3-acp-tm-gw.md): Deploy the API Control Plane, Traffic Manager, and Classic Gateway as separate Kubernetes workloads
- [K8s Pattern 4 - Fully Distributed Setup](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/install-and-setup/setup/kubernetes-deployment/kubernetes/am-pattern-4-acp-tm-gw-km.md): Fully distributed Kubernetes deployment with separate pods for Control Plane, Traffic Manager, Gateway, and Key Manager
- [K8s Pattern 5 - Simple Scalable with Dedicated Key Manager](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/install-and-setup/setup/kubernetes-deployment/kubernetes/am-pattern-5-all-in-one-gw-km.md): All-in-One plus Gateway deployment on Kubernetes with a separate Key Manager
- [K8s Pattern 6 - All-in-One with IS as Key Manager](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/install-and-setup/setup/kubernetes-deployment/kubernetes/am-pattern-6-all-in-one-is-as-km.md): Deploy All-in-One API Manager on Kubernetes with WSO2 Identity Server as the Key Manager
- [API Manager on OpenShift](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/install-and-setup/setup/kubernetes-deployment/openshift/openshift-deployment-overview.md): Deploy WSO2 API Manager on OpenShift — covers OpenShift-specific differences in security context, routing, and Helm values
- [API Manager on EKS](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/install-and-setup/setup/kubernetes-deployment/aws/deploying-wso2-api-m-on-eks.md): Deploy WSO2 API Manager on Amazon Elastic Kubernetes Service (EKS) using AWS RDS for databases and ALB for ingress
- [API Manager on AKS](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/install-and-setup/setup/kubernetes-deployment/azure/deploying-wso2-api-m-on-aks.md): Deploy WSO2 API Manager on Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS)
- [API Manager on GKE](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/install-and-setup/setup/kubernetes-deployment/gke/deploying-wso2-api-m-on-gke.md): Deploy WSO2 API Manager on Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE)
- [Production Deployment Checklist](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/install-and-setup/setup/deployment-best-practices/production-deployment-guidelines.md): Comprehensive checklist of configuration, security, and performance items to validate before going live
- [Security Guidelines for Production](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/install-and-setup/setup/deployment-best-practices/security-guidelines-for-production-deployment.md): Security hardening guidelines for runtime, OS, network, and data layers in a production deployment
- [Basic Health Checks](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/install-and-setup/setup/deployment-best-practices/basic-health-checks.md): Health check endpoints and port-level checks available for each API Manager component
- [Change the Hostname](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/install-and-setup/setup/deployment-best-practices/changing-the-hostname.md): Update the hostname used by API Manager components and the URLs exposed in the portals
- [Change the Default Ports](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/install-and-setup/setup/deployment-best-practices/changing-the-default-ports-with-offset.md): Use a port offset to run API Manager on non-default ports to avoid conflicts
- [Backup and Recovery](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/install-and-setup/setup/deployment-best-practices/backup-recovery.md): Backup and recovery strategy recommendations for API Manager databases, keystores, and configuration files
- [API Manager Performance Tuning](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/install-and-setup/setup/deployment-best-practices/tuning-performance.md): JVM, thread pool, connection pool, and transport-level tuning parameters for optimal API Manager throughput
- [Upgrade WSO2 API Manager](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/install-and-setup/upgrading-wso2-api-manager/upgrading-api-manager.md): Step-by-step guide for upgrading from a previous version of WSO2 API Manager to the latest release

## Tutorials

- [Tutorials Overview](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/tutorials/tutorials-overview.md): Index of all WSO2 API Manager tutorials — scenario-based and API management-focused walkthroughs
- [Scenario Overview](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/tutorials/scenarios/scenario-overview.md): Introduces the Union Station railway scenario used across all scenario tutorials, with setup prerequisites (Docker Compose)
- [Scenario 1 - Create a REST API from an OpenAPI Definition](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/tutorials/scenarios/scenario1-create-rest-api.md): Import an existing OpenAPI spec into the Publisher portal and publish it as a managed REST API
- [Scenario 2 - Engage Access Control to the API](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/tutorials/scenarios/scenario2-access-control.md): Apply role-based access control to restrict which users can see and subscribe to an API
- [Scenario 3 - Implement an API](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/tutorials/scenarios/scenario3-implementing-an-api.md): Configure a backend endpoint for a published API using the Publisher portal
- [Scenario 4 - Sign Up a New User](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/tutorials/scenarios/scenario4-user-signup-approval-flow.md): Enable and test the user self-registration workflow with admin approval on the Developer Portal
- [Scenario 5 - Get the Developer Community Involved](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/tutorials/scenarios/scenario5-developer-community-feature.md): Use Developer Portal community features — ratings, comments, and forums — to improve API engagement
- [Scenario 6 - Integrate with Data Sources](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/tutorials/scenarios/scenario6-integrating-with-data-sources.md): Expose a data source (database) as a managed API using WSO2 API Manager's data service integration
- [Scenario 7 - Analytics](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/tutorials/scenarios/scenario7-analytics.md): Enable and explore API analytics dashboards to monitor traffic, latency, and usage patterns
- [Scenario 8 - Rate Limiting](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/tutorials/scenarios/scenario8-rate-limiting.md): Apply and test subscription-level and API-level rate limiting policies on a published API
- [Scenario 9 - Realtime Data with WebSocket API](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/tutorials/scenarios/scenario9-realtime-data.md): Create and invoke a WebSocket Streaming API to deliver real-time data to consumers
- [Scenario 10 - Notifications Using WebHooks](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/tutorials/scenarios/scenario10-notifications-webhooks.md): Build and subscribe to a WebSub/WebHook API to receive event-driven notifications
- [Scenario 11 - GraphQL Support](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/tutorials/scenarios/scenario11-graphql.md): Create and invoke a GraphQL API managed through WSO2 API Manager
- [Scenario 12 - Guaranteed Message Delivery](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/tutorials/scenarios/scenario12-message-delivery.md): Use message broker integration to guarantee delivery of API messages even when the backend is unavailable
- [Scenario 13 - Integrate with Services via Connectors](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/tutorials/scenarios/scenario13-integrate-with-connectors.md): Use WSO2 Micro Integrator connectors to integrate APIs with third-party services
- [Scenario 14 - External Key Manager Support](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/tutorials/scenarios/scenario14-external-key-manager.md): Configure an external OAuth2 key manager and use it to generate tokens consumed by a managed API
- [Setting Up a Distributed Setup Using the APIM Enterprise Package](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/tutorials/create-distributed-setup-using-the-enterprise-package.md): Spin up a distributed WSO2 API Manager environment (Control Plane, Traffic Manager, Gateway) using Docker Compose and the Enterprise Package
- [Integrating API Manager with an External Broker and Gateway](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/tutorials/integrating-with-solace.md): Integrate a Solace event broker with WSO2 API Manager so Solace Event APIs appear as managed APIs in the Developer Portal
- [Develop an Integration from a Managed API](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/tutorials/develop-an-integration-with-a-managed-api.md): Top-down API-first workflow — design a REST API in API Manager first, then implement the backend integration with WSO2 Integration Studio
- [The Single Control Plane for Multiple Gateways](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/tutorials/single-control-plane-for-multiple-gateways.md): Configure and use a single WSO2 API Control Plane to manage multiple independent Gateway nodes
- [Federated API Gateway Deployment](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/tutorials/deploying-apis-to-federated-gateways-with-wso2.md): Deploy APIs from WSO2 API Manager to a federated third-party gateway (AWS API Gateway) using the federated gateway feature
- [Create and Publish a GraphQL API](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/tutorials/create-and-publish-a-graphql-api.md): End-to-end tutorial to design, publish, and invoke a GraphQL API in WSO2 API Manager
- [Create and Publish a WebSocket API](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/tutorials/streaming-api/create-and-publish-websocket-api.md): Build and invoke a WebSocket Streaming API using a chat application backend
- [Create and Publish a WebSub/WebHook API](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/tutorials/streaming-api/create-and-publish-websub-api.md): Create a WebSub/WebHook API that delivers GitHub issue creation notifications to registered subscribers
- [Create and Publish a SSE API](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/tutorials/streaming-api/create-and-publish-sse-api.md): Build and invoke a Server-Sent Events (SSE) API that streams live system memory usage data
- [Create and Publish an AWS Lambda API](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/tutorials/create-and-publish-awslambda-api.md): Expose an AWS Lambda function as a managed API through the WSO2 API Gateway
- [Create and Publish an API with Sequence as a Backend](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/tutorials/create-and-publish-a-sequencebackend-api.md): Use a Synapse mediation sequence as the backend for a REST API
- [Expose a SOAP Service as a REST API](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/tutorials/expose-a-soap-service-as-a-rest-api.md): Wrap an existing SOAP/WSDL service in a REST API facade using the Publisher portal
- [Edit an API by Modifying the API Definition](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/tutorials/edit-an-api-by-modifyng-the-api-definition.md): Update an existing API's OpenAPI definition directly in the Publisher portal to add or modify resources
- [Enforce Rate Limiting and Resource Access Policies](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/tutorials/enforce-throttling-and-resource-access-policies.md): Apply and test subscription-tier and resource-level rate limiting on an API end-to-end

## API Design & Manage

- [Design APIs Overview](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-design-manage/design/design-api-overview.md): Overview of all API design capabilities in the API Publisher — creation methods, API types, and management features
- [Create a REST API](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-design-manage/design/create-api/create-rest-api/create-a-rest-api.md): Create a REST API from scratch using the Publisher portal visual editor
- [Create a REST API from an OpenAPI Definition](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-design-manage/design/create-api/create-rest-api/create-a-rest-api-from-an-openapi-definition.md): Import an OpenAPI 2.0 or 3.0 specification to create a REST API in the Publisher portal
- [Expose a SOAP Service as a REST API](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-design-manage/design/create-api/create-rest-api/expose-a-soap-service-as-a-rest-api.md): Create a REST API facade over an existing SOAP/WSDL backend service
- [Generate REST API from SOAP Backend](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-design-manage/design/create-api/create-rest-api/generate-rest-api-from-soap-backend.md): Auto-generate a REST API from a WSDL definition by having API Manager map SOAP operations to REST resources
- [Test a REST API](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-design-manage/design/create-api/create-rest-api/test-a-rest-api.md): Use the built-in Publisher portal API console to test REST API resources before publishing
- [Create a GraphQL API](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-design-manage/design/create-api/create-a-graphql-api.md): Create a GraphQL API from a GraphQL schema definition (SDL) in the Publisher portal
- [Streaming API Overview](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-design-manage/design/create-api/create-streaming-api/streaming-api-overview.md): Overview of streaming API types supported — WebSocket, WebSub/WebHook, and SSE — and when to use each
- [Create a WebSocket API](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-design-manage/design/create-api/create-streaming-api/create-a-websocket-streaming-api.md): Create a WebSocket Streaming API in the Publisher portal, configure topics, and set up the backend
- [Create a WebSub/WebHook API](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-design-manage/design/create-api/create-streaming-api/create-a-websub-streaming-api.md): Create a WebSub/WebHook API for event-driven push notifications from a publisher to subscribers
- [Create a SSE API](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-design-manage/design/create-api/create-streaming-api/create-a-sse-streaming-api.md): Create a Server-Sent Events (SSE) API for server-to-client streaming of live data
- [Create a Streaming API from an AsyncAPI Definition](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-design-manage/design/create-api/create-streaming-api/create-a-streaming-api-from-an-asyncapi-definition.md): Import an AsyncAPI 2.x specification to create a WebSocket, WebSub, or SSE streaming API
- [Test a WebSub/WebHook API](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-design-manage/design/create-api/create-streaming-api/test-a-websub-api.md): Register and test a WebSub callback subscription to verify webhook delivery from the Publisher portal
- [Create an API Using a Service](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-design-manage/design/create-api/create-an-api-using-a-service.md): Create a managed API directly from a service registered in the Service Catalog, enabling API-first integration
- [Create an AI API](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-design-manage/design/create-api/create-ai-api/create-an-ai-api.md): Create an AI API to proxy and govern LLM provider endpoints (OpenAI, Azure OpenAI, etc.) through the AI Gateway
- [Create APIs with AI](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-design-manage/design/create-api/create-api-with-ai.md): Use AI assistance in the Publisher portal to generate API definitions and resources from natural language descriptions
- [Create API Revisions](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-design-manage/design/create-api/create-api-revisions.md): Create, manage, and deploy API revisions to safely test changes before promoting to production
- [Add Custom Properties to APIs](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-design-manage/design/create-api/adding-custom-properties-to-apis.md): Attach custom metadata properties to APIs for filtering, grouping, and governance
- [Change the Thumbnail of an API](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-design-manage/design/create-api/change-api-thumbnail.md): Upload a custom thumbnail image for an API to display on the Developer Portal listing
- [Prototype APIs Overview](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-design-manage/design/prototype-api/overview.md): Overview of prototype (pre-release) APIs — use cases for early feedback without a live backend
- [Create a Prototype API with Mock JS](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-design-manage/design/prototype-api/create-mocked-js-api.md): Create a prototype API with a JavaScript-based mock implementation running directly in the Classic Gateway
- [Prototype API with Existing Backend](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-design-manage/design/prototype-api/backend-url-prototype-api.md): Create a prototype API that forwards requests to an existing backend URL for early consumer testing
- [API Product Overview](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-design-manage/design/create-api-product/api-product-overview.md): What API Products are — bundling resources from multiple APIs into a single consumable product
- [Create an API Product](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-design-manage/design/create-api-product/create-api-product.md): Create an API Product by selecting and combining resources from one or more published APIs
- [Endpoint Types](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-design-manage/design/endpoints/endpoint-types.md): Reference for all supported endpoint types — HTTP/HTTPS, load-balanced, failover, dynamic, AWS Lambda, and mock
- [Endpoint Security - Basic Auth](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-design-manage/design/endpoints/endpoint-security/basic-auth.md): Configure HTTP Basic Authentication credentials for securing the gateway-to-backend connection
- [Endpoint Security - Digest Auth](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-design-manage/design/endpoints/endpoint-security/digest-auth.md): Configure HTTP Digest Authentication for the gateway-to-backend connection
- [Endpoint Security - OAuth 2.0](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-design-manage/design/endpoints/endpoint-security/oauth-2.0.md): Configure OAuth 2.0 client credentials to secure the gateway-to-backend connection
- [Endpoint Timeouts](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-design-manage/design/endpoints/resiliency/endpoint-timeouts.md): Configure connection and read timeout values for backend endpoints to handle slow or unresponsive services
- [Endpoint Suspension](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-design-manage/design/endpoints/resiliency/endpoint-suspension.md): Configure automatic endpoint suspension when error thresholds are exceeded and recovery conditions
- [Prevent API Suspension](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-design-manage/design/endpoints/resiliency/prevent-api-suspension.md): Configure settings to prevent an API from being automatically suspended even when its endpoint is temporarily failing
- [High Availability for Endpoints](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-design-manage/design/endpoints/high-availability-for-endpoints.md): Configure load-balanced or failover endpoint groups for high availability of backend services
- [Manage Certificates for Endpoints](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-design-manage/design/endpoints/certificates.md): Upload and manage TLS client certificates and CA certificates for mutual TLS backend connections
- [API Lifecycle](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-design-manage/design/lifecycle-management/api-lifecycle.md): Overview of the API lifecycle states — CREATED, PRE-RELEASED, PUBLISHED, BLOCKED, DEPRECATED, RETIRED — and valid transitions
- [Customize API Life Cycle](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-design-manage/design/lifecycle-management/customize-api-life-cycle.md): Modify the default API lifecycle to add custom states, checklist items, or transition permissions
- [Create a New API Version](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-design-manage/design/api-versioning/create-a-new-api-version.md): Create a new version of an existing API while retaining the original version's configuration
- [Deprecate the Old Version](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-design-manage/design/api-versioning/deprecate-the-old-version.md): Move the previous API version to the DEPRECATED state and optionally redirect subscribers to the new version
- [Backward Compatibility](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-design-manage/design/api-versioning/backward-compatibility.md): Configure the Default Version option so consumers can invoke an API without specifying the version number in the URL
- [Enable Notifications for API Versioning](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-design-manage/design/api-versioning/enabling-notifications.md): Configure email notifications to alert subscribers when an API is deprecated or a new version is published
- [Add API Documentation](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-design-manage/design/api-documentation/add-api-documentation.md): Attach inline content, URLs, or file uploads as documentation to an API visible in the Developer Portal
- [View Generated Documentation](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-design-manage/design/api-documentation/view-generated-documentation.md): View the API documentation automatically generated from the OpenAPI definition in the Developer Portal
- [Comment on an API via the Publisher](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-design-manage/design/api-collaborations/comment-on-an-api-via-the-publisher.md): Add and view comments on an API from the API Publisher portal for internal collaboration
- [Enable Social Media Interaction](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-design-manage/design/api-collaborations/enable-social-media-interaction.md): Enable social sharing buttons and community interaction features on Developer Portal API pages
- [API Policies Overview](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-design-manage/design/api-policies/overview.md): Overview of the API policies framework — attach request/response transformation and mediation policies to API operations
- [Attach Policy](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-design-manage/design/api-policies/attach-policy.md): Attach a built-in or custom policy to an API's request, response, or fault flow from the Publisher portal
- [Create Policy](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-design-manage/design/api-policies/create-policy.md): Create a reusable custom API policy from a Synapse mediation sequence template
- [Develop and Test Policies](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-design-manage/design/api-policies/develop-and-test-policies.md): Local development and testing workflow for custom API policies before deploying to API Manager
- [Set API Operation Rate Limits](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-design-manage/design/rate-limiting/set-api-level-throttling.md): Apply rate limiting policies at the API and resource operation level to control request throughput
- [Protect Backend Services](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-design-manage/design/rate-limiting/protect-backend-services.md): Configure backend rate limiting to protect upstream services from being overloaded by the gateway
- [Assign Business Plans](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-design-manage/design/rate-limiting/assign-business-plans.md): Assign subscription-tier business plans to an API to control consumer quota at the subscription level
- [Limit GraphQL Query Complexity](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-design-manage/design/rate-limiting/graphql-api/query-complexity-analysis.md): Configure maximum query complexity scores to prevent expensive GraphQL queries from overloading the backend
- [Limit GraphQL Query Depth](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-design-manage/design/rate-limiting/graphql-api/query-depth-analysis.md): Restrict the maximum nesting depth of incoming GraphQL queries to prevent deeply nested query attacks
- [Set Streaming API Rate Limits](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-design-manage/design/rate-limiting/set-streaming-api-limits.md): Configure rate limiting for WebSocket, WebSub, and SSE streaming API connections and events
- [Enable Publisher Access Control](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-design-manage/design/advanced-topics/enable-publisher-access-control-in-api-publisher-portal.md): Restrict which Publisher portal users can view or edit a specific API using role-based visibility controls
- [Control API Visibility and Subscription Availability](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-design-manage/design/advanced-topics/control-api-visibility-and-subscription-availability-in-developer-portal.md): Configure who can see and subscribe to an API in the Developer Portal — public, role-restricted, or tenant-restricted
- [Block Subscription to an API](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-design-manage/design/advanced-topics/block-subscription-to-an-api.md): Block a specific application's subscription to an API to revoke access without deleting the subscription
- [Disable Subscriptions for an API](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-design-manage/design/advanced-topics/disable-subscriptions-for-an-api.md): Disable the subscription requirement for an API to allow open (no-auth) access
- [Enable CORS for APIs](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-design-manage/design/advanced-topics/enabling-cors-for-apis.md): Configure Cross-Origin Resource Sharing (CORS) headers on an API to allow browser-based clients from other origins
- [Add an API State Change Workflow](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-design-manage/design/advanced-topics/adding-an-api-state-change-workflow.md): Add a human-approval workflow gate to API lifecycle state transitions (e.g., require admin approval to publish)
- [Validate API Definitions with Linters](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-design-manage/design/advanced-topics/validate-api-definitions-with-linters.md): Run Spectral-based linting rules against OpenAPI definitions in the Publisher portal to enforce API design standards
- [API Creator/Publisher Governance Capabilities](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-design-manage/governance/api-governance-api-creator-capabilities.md): Governance features available to API creators and publishers — compliance dashboards and ruleset violations in the Publisher portal
- [Deploy an API](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-design-manage/deploy-and-publish/deploy-on-gateway/deploy-api/deploy-an-api.md): Deploy an API revision to one or more Gateway environments from the Publisher portal
- [Expose APIs via Custom Hostnames](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-design-manage/deploy-and-publish/deploy-on-gateway/deploy-api/exposing-apis-via-custom-hostnames.md): Configure custom virtual hosts so APIs are accessible via custom domain names on the Gateway
- [Deploy Through Multiple API Gateways](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-design-manage/deploy-and-publish/deploy-on-gateway/deploy-api/deploy-through-multiple-api-gateways.md): Deploy the same API to multiple Gateway environments (e.g., production and sandbox) simultaneously
- [Revision Deployment Workflow](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-design-manage/deploy-and-publish/deploy-on-gateway/deploy-api/revision-deployment-workflow.md): Add an approval workflow gate to the API revision deployment step so deployments require admin sign-off
- [Publish an API to Developer Portal](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-design-manage/deploy-and-publish/publish-on-dev-portal/publish-an-api.md): Move an API to the PUBLISHED state so it appears in the Developer Portal for consumer discovery and subscription
- [Add a Third-party API](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-design-manage/deploy-and-publish/publish-on-dev-portal/third-party-api-support.md): List an externally hosted API in the Developer Portal without routing its traffic through the API Manager Gateway
- [Publish to Multiple External Developer Portals](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-design-manage/deploy-and-publish/publish-on-dev-portal/publish-to-multiple-external-api-stores.md): Federate API listings to external Developer Portals so APIs are discoverable in multiple API stores simultaneously
- [Import APIs from AWS API Gateway](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-design-manage/deploy-and-publish/publish-on-dev-portal/publish-aws-apis-in-the-dev-portal.md): Discover and import API definitions from AWS API Gateway into the WSO2 Developer Portal

## API Developer Portal

- [Publish an API to Developer Portal](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-developer-portal/publish-an-api-to-dev-portal.md): Guide to publishing APIs, AI APIs, MCP Servers, and API Products to the Developer Portal so consumers can discover and subscribe to them
- [Consume APIs Overview](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-developer-portal/consume-api-overview.md): Overview of the API consumer experience — how application developers discover, subscribe to, and invoke APIs from the Developer Portal
- [Search APIs](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-developer-portal/discover-apis/search.md): Search and filter APIs in the Developer Portal by name, tag, category, and other criteria
- [Marketplace Assistant](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-developer-portal/discover-apis/marketplace-assistant.md): AI-powered chat interface in the Developer Portal for discovering APIs through natural language queries and recommendations
- [Create Application](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-developer-portal/manage-application/create-application.md): Create a consumer application in the Developer Portal to subscribe to APIs and obtain OAuth2 credentials
- [Application Keys](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-developer-portal/manage-application/generate-keys/generate-api-keys.md): Generate consumer key/secret pairs and OAuth2 access tokens for an application, including support for multiple consumer secrets per application
- [Overview of Access Tokens](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-developer-portal/manage-application/generate-keys/obtain-access-token/overview-of-access-tokens.md): Explanation of JWT and opaque access token types and how they are used to authenticate API requests
- [Access Tokens Per Device](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-developer-portal/manage-application/generate-keys/obtain-access-token/access-tokens-per-device.md): Configure applications to issue separate access tokens per device for granular session control
- [Change the Default Token Expiration Time](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-developer-portal/manage-application/generate-keys/obtain-access-token/changing-the-default-token-expiration-time.md): Adjust the default lifetime of OAuth2 access tokens and refresh tokens
- [Revoke OAuth2 Application](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-developer-portal/manage-application/generate-keys/revoke-oauth2-application.md): Revoke all active OAuth2 tokens for an application to immediately cut off its API access
- [Share Applications](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-developer-portal/manage-application/sharing-applications/sharing-applications.md): Share a Developer Portal application with other users in the same organization so they can view and use it
- [Add Custom Attributes to Applications](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-developer-portal/manage-application/advanced-topics/add-custom-attributes-to-applications.md): Attach administrator-defined custom metadata fields to applications for business context
- [Change the Owner of an Application](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-developer-portal/manage-application/advanced-topics/changing-the-owner-of-an-application.md): Transfer ownership of a Developer Portal application to another user account
- [Upgrade the Token Type to JWT](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-developer-portal/manage-application/advanced-topics/upgrade-token-type-of-application-to-jwt.md): Migrate an existing application from opaque tokens to self-contained JWT access tokens
- [Change the Provider of an API](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-developer-portal/manage-application/advanced-topics/changing-the-provider-of-an-api.md): Transfer ownership of a published API to a different user account in the Publisher portal
- [Add an Application Creation Workflow](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-developer-portal/manage-application/advanced-topics/adding-an-application-creation-workflow.md): Require admin approval before new Developer Portal applications are activated
- [Add an Application Update Workflow](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-developer-portal/manage-application/advanced-topics/adding-an-application-update-workflow.md): Require admin approval before updates to existing Developer Portal applications take effect
- [Add an Application Key Generation Workflow](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-developer-portal/manage-application/advanced-topics/adding-an-application-key-generation-workflow.md): Require admin approval before OAuth2 keys are generated for an application
- [Subscribe to an API](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-developer-portal/manage-subscription/subscribe-to-an-api.md): Subscribe an application to a published API under a selected business plan to obtain access tokens for invoking it
- [Add an API Subscription Workflow](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-developer-portal/manage-subscription/advanced-topics/adding-an-api-subscription-workflow.md): Require admin approval before an API subscription becomes active
- [Add an API Subscription Tier Update Workflow](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-developer-portal/manage-subscription/advanced-topics/adding-an-api-subscription-tier-update-workflow.md): Require admin approval when a subscriber upgrades or downgrades their subscription tier
- [Add an API Subscription Deletion Workflow](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-developer-portal/manage-subscription/advanced-topics/adding-an-api-subscription-deletion-workflow.md): Require admin approval before an API subscription is deleted
- [Test a REST API via API Console](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-developer-portal/invoke-apis/invoke-apis-using-tools/invoke-an-api-using-the-integrated-api-console.md): Interactively invoke REST API resources from the Developer Portal using the built-in OpenAPI console
- [Test a GraphQL API via Console](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-developer-portal/invoke-apis/invoke-apis-using-tools/invoke-an-graphql-api-using-the-integrated-graphql-console.md): Test a GraphQL API from the Developer Portal using the built-in GraphQL console
- [Add Additional Headers in API Console](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-developer-portal/invoke-apis/invoke-apis-using-tools/include-additional-headers-in-the-api-console.md): Inject custom HTTP headers into test requests made from the Developer Portal API console
- [Test APIs via SOAP Client](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-developer-portal/invoke-apis/invoke-apis-using-tools/invoke-an-api-using-a-soap-client.md): Invoke a SOAP-passthrough API using an external SOAP client tool
- [Test APIs with Postman](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-developer-portal/invoke-apis/invoke-apis-using-tools/try-out-using-postman.md): Export an API's OpenAPI definition from the Developer Portal and test it in Postman
- [Test APIs with API Chat](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-developer-portal/invoke-apis/invoke-apis-using-tools/test-apis-with-apichat.md): Test REST APIs using natural language through the AI-powered API Chat feature in the Developer Portal
- [View API Documents](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-developer-portal/view-api-documentation/view-api-documentation.md): Browse API documentation and attached files published by API creators in the Developer Portal
- [Interact with the Community](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-developer-portal/collaboration/interact-with-the-community.md): Use Developer Portal community features — ratings, comments, and forums — to engage with API providers and other consumers
- [Generate SDKs in Developer Portal](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-developer-portal/generating-sdks/generate-sdks-in-dev-portal.md): Generate client SDKs for a published API in multiple programming languages from the Developer Portal
- [Write a Client Application Using the SDK](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-developer-portal/generating-sdks/write-a-client-application-using-the-sdk.md): Use a generated SDK to build a client application that invokes an API
- [Recover Password](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-developer-portal/user-account-management/recover-password.md): Recover a forgotten Developer Portal account password via email verification
- [Change Password](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-developer-portal/user-account-management/change-dev-portal-password.md): Change the logged-in user's password from within the Developer Portal
- [Rate Limiting for App Developers](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-developer-portal/rate-limiting/rate-limiting-for-app-developers.md): How application-level and subscription-level rate limiting works from the consumer's perspective
- [Manage Application Rate Limits](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-developer-portal/rate-limiting/manage-application-rate-limits.md): View and manage the rate limiting tiers applied to your Developer Portal applications
- [Handle Rate Limiting Errors](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-developer-portal/rate-limiting/handle-rate-limiting-errors.md): Understand and handle HTTP 429 rate limiting responses returned by the API Gateway
- [Reset Application Throttling Policies](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-developer-portal/rate-limiting/resetting-application-throttling-policies.md): Reset the request counter for an application subscription to restore access after hitting a rate limit
- [B2B API Consumption](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-developer-portal/b2b-api-consumption/api-consumption.md): Organization-based API access control in the Developer Portal — restrict API visibility and subscriptions to specific business organizations
- [Setup WSO2 Identity Server as Federated Authenticator for B2B](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-developer-portal/b2b-api-consumption/setup-identity-server.md): Configure WSO2 Identity Server as the federated identity provider for B2B organization-based Developer Portal access

## API Security

- [Configure API Security Audit](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-security/design-time/configuring-api-security-audit.md): Integrate with 42Crunch to run OpenAPI security audits on API definitions from the Publisher portal and receive a security score
- [API Authentication Overview](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-security/runtime/api-authentication/api-authentication-overview.md): Overview of all authentication mechanisms supported by the API Gateway — OAuth2, API Key, Basic Auth, Mutual SSL, and JWT
- [Disable Security](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-security/runtime/api-authentication/disable-security.md): Configure an API to allow unauthenticated (open) access by disabling the authentication handler
- [Secure APIs Using API Keys](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-security/runtime/api-authentication/secure-apis-using-api-keys.md): Configure and use self-contained API keys for lightweight, stateless API authentication
- [Legacy API Keys (DEPRECATED)](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-security/runtime/api-authentication/secure-apis-using-legacy-api-keys.md): Documentation for the deprecated opaque API key authentication mechanism for applications migrating from older versions
- [Secure APIs Using Basic Authentication](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-security/runtime/api-authentication/secure-apis-using-basic-authentication.md): Configure HTTP Basic Authentication for API access using username and password credentials
- [Secure APIs Using Mutual SSL](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-security/runtime/api-authentication/secure-apis-using-mutual-ssl.md): Configure mutual TLS (mTLS) client certificate authentication for APIs
- [Secure APIs Using OAuth2 Tokens](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-security/runtime/api-authentication/secure-apis-using-oauth2-tokens.md): Configure OAuth2 bearer token authentication for APIs including grant type flows and token validation
- [Securing APIs Using Certificate Bound Access Tokens](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-security/runtime/api-authentication/securing-apis-using-certificate-bound-access-tokens.md): Bind OAuth2 access tokens to client TLS certificates (mutual TLS token binding) to prevent token theft
- [API Authorization Overview](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-security/runtime/authorization/api-authorization.md): Overview of fine-grained authorization options — OAuth2 scopes, XACML, and application scopes
- [Role-based Access Control Using XACML](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-security/runtime/authorization/role-based-access-control-using-xacml.md): Enforce attribute-based access control on API requests using an external XACML policy engine
- [Fine-grained Access Control with OAuth Scopes](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-security/runtime/authorization/oauth2-scopes/fine-grained-access-control-with-oauth-scopes.md): Restrict access to individual API resources using OAuth2 scopes mapped to user roles
- [Application Scopes](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-security/runtime/authorization/oauth2-scopes/application-scopes.md): Define and use application-level OAuth2 scopes that restrict what an application can access regardless of the user's roles
- [Scope Whitelisting](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-security/runtime/authorization/oauth2-scopes/scope-whitelisting.md): Whitelist OAuth2 scopes so they are passed through to the backend without validation by the Gateway
- [JSON Schema Validator](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-security/runtime/api-request-response-schema-validation/json-schema-validator.md): Enable JSON Schema validation on API request and response payloads against the OpenAPI definition
- [OPA Validation Overview](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-security/runtime/opa-validation/overview.md): Use Open Policy Agent (OPA) to evaluate complex authorization policies on incoming API requests at the Gateway
- [Custom OPA Policy for Classic Gateway](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-security/runtime/opa-validation/custom-opa-policy-for-regular-gateway.md): Write and deploy a custom OPA Rego policy to authorize API requests on the Classic Gateway
- [Key Manager Overview](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-security/key-management/key-manager-overview.md): Overview of the WSO2 Key Manager — the OAuth2 authorization server used for token issuance, validation, and application registration
- [OAuth2 Grant Types Overview](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-security/key-management/authentication/grant-types/overview.md): Overview of all OAuth2 grant types supported — Password, Client Credentials, Authorization Code, Refresh Token, JWT, SAML, Kerberos, and NTLM
- [Password Grant](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-security/key-management/authentication/grant-types/password-grant.md): Use the OAuth2 Resource Owner Password Credentials grant to obtain tokens with a username and password
- [Client Credentials Grant](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-security/key-management/authentication/grant-types/client-credentials-grant.md): Use the OAuth2 Client Credentials grant for machine-to-machine API authentication
- [Authorization Code Grant](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-security/key-management/authentication/grant-types/authorization-code-grant.md): Use the OAuth2 Authorization Code grant for user-delegated access with browser-based login flows
- [Refresh Token Grant](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-security/key-management/authentication/grant-types/refresh-token-grant.md): Use refresh tokens to obtain new access tokens without re-authenticating the user
- [JWT Grant](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-security/key-management/authentication/grant-types/jwt-grant.md): Exchange a JWT assertion for an OAuth2 access token using the JWT Bearer grant type
- [SAML Extension Grant](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-security/key-management/authentication/grant-types/saml-extension-grant.md): Exchange a SAML2 assertion for an OAuth2 access token to bridge SAML-based SSO with OAuth2 APIs
- [Kerberos OAuth2 Grant](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-security/key-management/authentication/grant-types/kerberos-oauth2-grant.md): Use a Kerberos ticket to obtain an OAuth2 access token for Windows Integrated Authentication scenarios
- [NTLM Grant](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-security/key-management/authentication/grant-types/ntlm-grant.md): Use NTLM credentials to obtain OAuth2 tokens in Windows domain environments
- [JWT Tokens](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-security/key-management/tokens/jwt-tokens.md): How self-contained JWT access tokens are structured, signed, and validated at the Gateway
- [Token Expiration](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-security/key-management/tokens/token-expiration.md): Configure access token and refresh token expiry times at the application and global level
- [Token Persistence](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-security/key-management/tokens/token-persistence.md): Configure whether tokens are persisted in the database and the trade-offs between persistence modes
- [Token Revocation](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-security/key-management/tokens/token-revocation.md): Revoke OAuth2 access and refresh tokens and propagate revocation events across Gateway nodes
- [Encrypting OAuth2 Tokens](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-security/key-management/tokens/encrypting-oauth2-tokens.md): Enable encryption of OAuth2 tokens stored in the database to protect them at rest
- [Hashing OAuth Keys](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-security/key-management/tokens/hashing-oauth-keys.md): Store consumer keys, consumer secrets, and access tokens as hashes rather than plain text in the database
- [Multiple Active Access Tokens](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-security/key-management/tokens/multiple-active-access-tokens.md): Allow an application to hold multiple valid access tokens simultaneously for the same scope set
- [Securing OAuth Token with HMAC Validation](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-security/key-management/tokens/securing-oauth-token-with-hmac-validation.md): Add HMAC signature validation on OAuth2 tokens to detect tampering
- [Provisioning Out-of-Band OAuth Clients](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-security/key-management/applications/provisioning-out-of-band-oauth-clients.md): Register OAuth2 client applications that were created outside of API Manager into the key management system
- [Federating OAuth Applications](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-security/key-management/applications/federating-oauth-applications.md): Map externally registered OAuth2 applications to API Manager subscriptions for federated access
- [Obtain User Profile Information with OpenID Connect](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-security/key-management/identity/obtaining-user-profile-information-with-openid-connect.md): Retrieve user profile claims from the OIDC UserInfo endpoint using access tokens issued by the Key Manager
- [Third-Party Key Manager Overview](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-security/key-management/third-party-key-managers/overview.md): Overview of configuring external OAuth2 authorization servers as Key Managers alongside or instead of the built-in Key Manager
- [Configure WSO2 IS 7.x as Key Manager](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-security/key-management/third-party-key-managers/configure-wso2is7-connector.md): Connect WSO2 Identity Server 7.x as a Key Manager for token issuance and validation
- [Configure WSO2 IS 6.x as Key Manager](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-security/key-management/third-party-key-managers/configure-wso2is-connector.md): Connect WSO2 Identity Server 6.x as a Key Manager
- [Configure Asgardeo as Key Manager](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-security/key-management/third-party-key-managers/configure-asgardeo-connector.md): Use Asgardeo (WSO2's cloud identity service) as the OAuth2 Key Manager
- [Configure Keycloak as Key Manager](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-security/key-management/third-party-key-managers/configure-keycloak-connector.md): Integrate Keycloak as an external OAuth2 Key Manager
- [Configure Okta as Key Manager](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-security/key-management/third-party-key-managers/configure-okta-connector.md): Integrate Okta as an external OAuth2 Key Manager
- [Configure Auth0 as Key Manager](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-security/key-management/third-party-key-managers/configure-auth0-connector.md): Integrate Auth0 as an external OAuth2 Key Manager
- [Configure PingFederate as Key Manager](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-security/key-management/third-party-key-managers/configure-pingfederate-connector.md): Integrate PingFederate as an external OAuth2 Key Manager
- [Configure ForgeRock as Key Manager](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-security/key-management/third-party-key-managers/configure-forgerock-connector.md): Integrate ForgeRock Access Management as an external OAuth2 Key Manager
- [Configure Azure AD as Key Manager](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-security/key-management/third-party-key-managers/configure-azure-ad-key-manager.md): Use Azure Active Directory as an external OAuth2 Key Manager
- [Configure a Custom Key Manager for Out-of-Band Provisioning](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-security/key-management/third-party-key-managers/configure-custom-km-out-of-band.md): Integrate any custom OAuth2 authorization server as a Key Manager with out-of-band application provisioning
- [Configure a Custom Key Manager](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-security/key-management/third-party-key-managers/configure-custom-connector.md): Write and register a fully custom Key Manager connector for a non-standard OAuth2 authorization server
- [Configure the Global Key Manager](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-security/key-management/third-party-key-managers/configure-global-key-manager.md): Configure a Key Manager to operate globally across all tenants in a multi-tenant API Manager deployment
- [Application Configuration Constraints](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-security/key-management/third-party-key-managers/configure-km-application-configuration-constraints.md): Define constraints on how developers can configure OAuth2 application settings for a specific Key Manager

## API Gateway

- [Gateway Overview](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-gateway/platform-gateway-capability.md): Overview of the two gateway types available — Platform Gateway (lightweight, hybrid) and Classic Gateway (Universal, Synapse-based) — and how they integrate with the API Manager Control Plane
- [Platform Gateway - Getting Started](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-gateway/platform-gateway/getting-started.md): Set up a Platform Gateway runtime and connect it to the API Manager Control Plane for hybrid deployments
- [Platform Gateway - Setting Up](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-gateway/platform-gateway/setting-up.md): Configure environment, certificates, and Policy Hub integration for a Platform Gateway instance
- [Platform Gateway - Adding and Managing Policies](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-gateway/platform-gateway/adding-and-managing-policies.md): Apply and manage gateway policies on APIs running on the Platform Gateway
- [Classic Gateway Overview](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-gateway/overview-of-the-api-gateway.md): Overview of the Classic Gateway (Universal) — Synapse-based runtime that intercepts, secures, and routes API traffic
- [Deploy an API to Classic Gateway](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-gateway/deploy-api-to-gateway.md): Deploy an API revision to the Classic Gateway runtime from the Publisher portal
- [Response Caching](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-gateway/response-caching.md): Enable response caching in the Classic Gateway to reduce backend load for idempotent API responses
- [Pass End User Attributes to the Backend](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-gateway/passing-enduser-attributes-to-the-backend-via-api-gateway.md): Forward authenticated user information (JWT claims) from the Gateway to the backend service via headers
- [Gateway Environments](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-gateway/maintaining-separate-production-and-sandbox-gateways.md): Configure and maintain separate production and sandbox Gateway environments for safe API testing
- [Scale the Gateway](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-gateway/scaling-the-gateway.md): Horizontal scaling strategies and configuration for the Classic Gateway cluster
- [Classic Gateway with Dedicated Tenants](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-gateway/maintain-seperate-gateways-per-tenants.md): Deploy dedicated Classic Gateway instances for individual tenants in a multi-tenant setup
- [Classic Gateways with Dedicated Backends](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-gateway/api-gateways-with-dedicated-backends.md): Route traffic from a specific Gateway node to a dedicated backend service pool
- [Mutual SSL Between Gateway and Backend](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-gateway/mutual-ssl-between-api-gateway-and-backend.md): Configure mutual TLS between the Classic Gateway and the backend service for outbound connection security
- [Storing Custom Synapse Artifacts](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-gateway/custom-synapse-artifacts.md): Deploy and manage custom Synapse sequences, endpoints, and message stores in the Classic Gateway
- [Adding Dynamic Endpoints](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-gateway/policies/adding-dynamic-endpoints.md): Use a mediation policy to dynamically select the backend endpoint at runtime based on message content
- [Adding a Class Mediator](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-gateway/policies/adding-a-class-mediator.md): Write and deploy a custom Java class mediator to perform custom message processing in the Classic Gateway
- [Adding a Non-Blocking Send Operation](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-gateway/policies/adding-a-non-blocking-send-operation.md): Create a custom mediation policy using the non-blocking Send mediator for VFS transport scenarios — removes the ClientApiNonBlocking property to handle large file transfers in a single thread without out-of-memory errors
- [Configuring Message Builders and Formatters](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-gateway/policies/configuring-message-builders-formatters.md): Configure message builders and formatters for handling non-standard content types in the Classic Gateway
- [Disabling Message Chunking](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-gateway/policies/disabling-message-chunking.md): Disable chunked transfer encoding on requests sent to the backend for APIs whose legacy backends do not support chunked messages
- [JWT Claim Based Access Validator](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-gateway/policies/jwt-claim-based-access-validator.md): Enforce access control by validating specific claims in the incoming JWT token
- [Mapping Backend URLs with Publisher URLs](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-gateway/policies/mapping-the-parameters-of-your-backend-urls-with-the-api-publisher-urls.md): Rewrite the URL path from the consumer-facing API URL to the backend service URL
- [Passing Custom Authorization Token to Backend](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-gateway/policies/passing-a-custom-authorization-token-to-the-backend.md): Forward a custom authorization token from the Gateway to the backend rather than the consumer's access token
- [Removing Specific Request Headers from Response](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-gateway/policies/removing-specific-request-headers-from-response.md): Strip internal or sensitive request headers from the response before returning it to the client
- [Revoke One Time Tokens Policy](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-gateway/policies/revoke-one-time-tokens-policy.md): Use one-time-use tokens that are invalidated immediately after the first successful API invocation
- [Transforming API Message Payload](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-gateway/policies/transforming-api-message-payload.md): Understand how the Classic Gateway transforms API request and response payloads using message builders and formatters — covers JSON and XML content type handling and conversion between formats
- [Global Gateway Policies](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-gateway/gateway-policies.md): Deploy and manage global-level Synapse policies that apply to all APIs on a specific Classic Gateway node
- [Gateway Threat Protectors Overview](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-gateway/threat-protectors/gateway-threat-protectors-for-api-manager.md): Overview of the three gateway threat protection types — regex, JSON, and XML
- [Regular Expression Threat Protection](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-gateway/threat-protectors/regular-expression-threat-protection-for-api-gateway.md): Detect and block API requests containing patterns that match configured regular expressions
- [JSON Threat Protection](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-gateway/threat-protectors/json-threat-protection-for-api-gateway.md): Validate JSON payloads against size and structural limits to prevent JSON-based attacks
- [XML Threat Protection](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-gateway/threat-protectors/xml-threat-protection-for-api-gateway.md): Validate XML payloads against configured limits to prevent XML bomb and entity expansion attacks
- [Bot Detection](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-gateway/threat-protectors/bot-detection.md): Detect and alert on API requests originating from known bot IP addresses or invalid API keys
- [Understand Rate Limit Enforcement](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-gateway/rate-limiting/understand-rate-limit-enforcement.md): How the Classic Gateway evaluates multiple rate limiting tiers in real time and coordinates with the Traffic Manager
- [Enforce GraphQL Query Limits](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-gateway/rate-limiting/enforce-graphql-query-limits.md): Apply and enforce query complexity and depth limits on GraphQL API requests at the Gateway
- [Enforce Streaming API Limits](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-gateway/rate-limiting/enforce-streaming-api-limits.md): Apply rate limits to WebSocket connections and event throughput at the Classic Gateway
- [Enforce Custom Throttling](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-gateway/rate-limiting/enforce-custom-throttling.md): Write custom Siddhi throttling policies to implement complex rate limiting logic beyond the built-in tiers
- [Configure Distributed Burst Control and Backend Rate Limiting](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-gateway/rate-limiting/configuring-rate-limiting-api-gateway-cluster.md): Configure burst control and backend rate limiting across a Classic Gateway cluster
- [Configure Distributed Throttling](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-gateway/rate-limiting/distributed-throttling.md): Set up distributed rate limiting across multiple Gateway nodes using the Traffic Manager
- [Federated Gateways Overview](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-gateway/federated-gateways/overview.md): Overview of deploying APIs to third-party federated gateways (AWS, Azure, Kong, Envoy) from the WSO2 API Manager control plane
- [Deploy on AWS API Gateway](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-gateway/federated-gateways/aws/deploy-on-aws-api-gateway.md): Configure AWS API Gateway as a federated gateway and deploy WSO2-managed APIs to it
- [Discover APIs on AWS API Gateway](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-gateway/federated-gateways/aws/discover-apis-on-aws-api-gateway.md): Import and catalog APIs already deployed on AWS API Gateway into the WSO2 Developer Portal
- [Deploy on Azure API Gateway](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-gateway/federated-gateways/azure/deploy-on-azure-api-gateway.md): Configure Azure API Management as a federated gateway and deploy WSO2-managed APIs to it
- [Discover APIs on Azure API Gateway](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-gateway/federated-gateways/azure/discover-apis-on-azure-api-gateway.md): Import and catalog APIs deployed on Azure API Management into the WSO2 Developer Portal
- [Discover APIs on Envoy Gateway (Kubernetes)](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-gateway/federated-gateways/envoygateway/eg-k8s/discover-apis-on-eg-gateway-in-kubernetes.md): Import and catalog APIs from an Envoy Gateway running in Kubernetes into the WSO2 Developer Portal
- [Discover APIs on Kong Gateway (Kubernetes)](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-gateway/federated-gateways/kong/kong-kubernetes/discover-apis-on-kong-gateway-in-kubernetes.md): Import and catalog APIs from Kong Gateway running in Kubernetes into the WSO2 Developer Portal
