- [Discover APIs on Kong Gateway (Standalone)](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-gateway/federated-gateways/kong/kong-standalone/discover-apis-on-kong-gateway.md): Import and catalog APIs from a standalone Kong Gateway instance into the WSO2 Developer Portal
- [Configure a Custom Gateway Agent](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/api-gateway/federated-gateways/configure-custom-gateway-agent.md): Implement and register a custom gateway agent to federate any third-party API gateway not natively supported

## AI Gateway

- [AI Gateway Overview](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/ai-gateway/ai-gateway-overview.md): Overview of the WSO2 AI Gateway — enterprise-grade infrastructure for managing LLM APIs and MCP servers with cost control, security, and observability
- [Getting Started with LLM Gateway](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/ai-gateway/getting-started-with-ai-gateway.md): Create your first AI API to proxy an LLM provider (OpenAI, Azure OpenAI, etc.) through the AI Gateway
- [AI Backend Security](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/ai-gateway/ai-backend-security.md): Configure secure credential management for AI service provider connections through the AI Gateway
- [AI Rate Limiting](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/ai-gateway/rate-limiting.md): Apply token-based and request-based rate limiting policies to AI APIs to control LLM usage and costs
- [Multi-Model Routing Overview](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/ai-gateway/multi-model-routing/overview.md): Overview of multi-model routing strategies — load balancing and failover across AI models within the same provider
- [Load Balancing](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/ai-gateway/multi-model-routing/load-balancing.md): Distribute LLM requests evenly across multiple AI models using round-robin load balancing
- [Failover](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/ai-gateway/multi-model-routing/failover.md): Automatically redirect LLM traffic to a fallback model when the primary model is unavailable
- [Intelligent Model Routing](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/ai-gateway/multi-model-routing/intelligent-model-routing.md): Route LLM requests to different models based on the meaning and intent of the prompt — uses a configured LLM classifier to match each request against predefined routing rules with context descriptions
- [Semantic Model Routing](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/ai-gateway/multi-model-routing/semantic-model-routing.md): Route LLM requests to different models based on the semantic content of the prompt
- [AI Service Provider Ecosystem Overview](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/ai-gateway/ai-vendor-management/overview.md): Overview of the pre-built connectors for major AI providers — OpenAI, Azure OpenAI, AWS Bedrock, Anthropic, Gemini, Mistral
- [Anthropic Provider](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/ai-gateway/ai-vendor-management/anthropic.md): Configure an Anthropic (Claude) AI service provider connection in the AI Gateway
- [AWS Bedrock Provider](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/ai-gateway/ai-vendor-management/aws-bedrock.md): Configure an AWS Bedrock AI service provider connection in the AI Gateway
- [Azure AI Foundry Provider](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/ai-gateway/ai-vendor-management/azure-ai-foundry.md): Configure an Azure AI Foundry service provider connection in the AI Gateway
- [Azure OpenAI Provider](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/ai-gateway/ai-vendor-management/azure-openai.md): Configure an Azure OpenAI service provider connection in the AI Gateway
- [Gemini Provider](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/ai-gateway/ai-vendor-management/gemini.md): Configure a Google Gemini AI service provider connection in the AI Gateway
- [Mistral AI Provider](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/ai-gateway/ai-vendor-management/mistral-ai.md): Configure a Mistral AI service provider connection in the AI Gateway
- [OpenAI Provider](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/ai-gateway/ai-vendor-management/openai.md): Configure an OpenAI service provider connection in the AI Gateway
- [Custom AI Service Providers Overview](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/ai-gateway/ai-vendor-management/custom-ai-vendors/overview.md): Overview of integrating non-standard AI providers as custom AI service providers
- [Custom AI Provider Connector](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/ai-gateway/ai-vendor-management/custom-ai-vendors/custom-connector.md): Build a custom connector to integrate any AI provider that does not have a built-in adapter
- [Onboarding a Custom AI Service Provider](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/ai-gateway/ai-vendor-management/custom-ai-vendors/onboarding-custom-ai-service-provider.md): Step-by-step guide to register and configure a custom AI service provider in the Admin Portal
- [Prompt Management Overview](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/ai-gateway/prompt-management/overview.md): Overview of prompt management capabilities — standardize, secure, and enforce prompt structures across all LLM API traffic
- [Prompt Decorator](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/ai-gateway/prompt-management/prompt-decorator.md): Prepend or append system-level instructions to every LLM request passing through the AI Gateway
- [Prompt Template](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/ai-gateway/prompt-management/prompt-template.md): Enforce a structured prompt format for all requests sent through an AI API
- [AI Guardrails Overview](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/ai-gateway/ai-guardrails/overview.md): Overview of AI guardrails for real-time enforcement of safety, compliance, and content policies on LLM API traffic
- [Content Length Guardrail](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/ai-gateway/ai-guardrails/content-length-guardrail.md): Enforce byte-length limits on LLM request and response payloads
- [Regex Guardrail](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/ai-gateway/ai-guardrails/regex-guardrail.md): Block or allow LLM requests and responses that match a configured regular expression pattern
- [JSON Schema Guardrail](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/ai-gateway/ai-guardrails/json-schema-guardrail.md): Validate LLM response payloads against a JSON schema definition
- [Sentence Count Guardrail](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/ai-gateway/ai-guardrails/sentence-count-guardrail.md): Enforce minimum and maximum sentence count limits on LLM inputs and outputs
- [URL Guardrail](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/ai-gateway/ai-guardrails/url-guardrail.md): Detect and block URLs in LLM prompts or completions
- [Word Count Guardrail](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/ai-gateway/ai-guardrails/word-count-guardrail.md): Enforce minimum and maximum word count limits on LLM inputs and outputs
- [Semantic Prompt Guardrail](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/ai-gateway/ai-guardrails/semantic-prompt-guardrail.md): Block semantically similar prompts using embedding-based similarity matching against allow/deny phrase lists
- [Semantic Tool Filtering Guardrail](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/ai-gateway/ai-guardrails/semantic-tool-filtering-guardrail.md): Filter MCP tool calls based on semantic similarity to allowed or blocked tool descriptions
- [PII Masking with Regex](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/ai-gateway/ai-guardrails/regex-pii-masking.md): Mask personally identifiable information in LLM requests and responses using configurable regex patterns
- [Azure Content Safety Guardrail](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/ai-gateway/ai-guardrails/azure-content-safety.md): Integrate Azure Content Safety to detect and filter hate speech, sexual content, violence, and self-harm in LLM traffic
- [AWS Bedrock Guardrail](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/ai-gateway/ai-guardrails/aws-bedrock-guardrail.md): Integrate AWS Bedrock Guardrails for enterprise-grade content filtering, topic blocking, and PII protection on LLM traffic
- [Guardrail Error Response](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/ai-gateway/ai-guardrails/guardrail-error-response.md): Understand and customize the error response format returned when an AI guardrail blocks a request
- [Semantic Caching](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/ai-gateway/semantic-caching.md): Cache LLM responses by semantic similarity to reduce latency and AI provider costs for repeated or similar queries
- [AI APIs via SDKs](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/ai-gateway/using-proxy-apis-in-sdks.md): Use LangChain and provider-specific Python SDKs to call AI APIs managed by the WSO2 AI Gateway
- [Claude Code with AI Gateway](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/ai-gateway/ai-tools/claude-code-with-ai-gateway.md): Configure Claude Code to route all LLM traffic through the WSO2 AI Gateway for governance and observability
- [Google Gemini CLI with AI Gateway](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/ai-gateway/ai-tools/gemini-cli-with-ai-gateway.md): Configure the Google Gemini CLI to route LLM traffic through the WSO2 AI Gateway
- [OpenAI Codex CLI with AI Gateway](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/ai-gateway/ai-tools/codex-with-ai-gateway.md): Configure the OpenAI Codex CLI to route LLM traffic through the WSO2 AI Gateway
- [MCP Gateway - Getting Started](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/ai-gateway/mcp-gateway/overview.md): Overview of MCP Gateway capabilities — create, manage, and govern MCP servers from OpenAPI definitions, existing APIs, or upstream MCP servers
- [Create MCP Server from an OpenAPI Definition](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/ai-gateway/mcp-gateway/create-from-openapi.md): Generate a governed MCP server from an OpenAPI specification so REST APIs become discoverable AI tools
- [Create MCP Server from an Existing API](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/ai-gateway/mcp-gateway/create-from-api.md): Convert an existing API already published in API Manager into a governed MCP server
- [Proxy an Existing MCP Server](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/ai-gateway/mcp-gateway/create-from-mcp-server.md): Route and govern traffic to an upstream remote MCP server through the WSO2 MCP Gateway
- [Update and Deploy a MCP Server](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/ai-gateway/mcp-gateway/update-and-deploy-mcp-server.md): Update an existing MCP server's tool schemas or policies and deploy the changes
- [Subscribe to a MCP Server](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/ai-gateway/mcp-gateway/subscribe-to-a-mcp-server.md): Subscribe an application to a published MCP server and obtain credentials to invoke it
- [Consume MCP Servers from MCP Hub](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/ai-gateway/mcp-gateway/invoke-a-mcp-server-using-playground.md): Discover and invoke MCP servers from the Developer Portal MCP Hub using the built-in playground

## API Analytics & Monetization

- [API Analytics Overview](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/monitoring/api-analytics/analytics-overview.md): Overview of API Manager analytics — usage metrics, performance data, and integration options for monitoring API traffic
- [Moesif Integration Guide](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/monitoring/api-analytics/moesif-analytics/moesif-integration-guide.md): Connect WSO2 API Manager to Moesif for advanced API analytics, usage tracking, and monetization
- [Moesif Analytics Dashboards](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/monitoring/api-analytics/moesif-analytics/moesif-analytics-dashboards.md): Explore the Moesif dashboards available after integrating with WSO2 API Manager
- [ELK-Based Analytics Installation Guide](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/monitoring/api-analytics/on-prem/elk-installation-guide.md): Set up the ELK stack (Elasticsearch, Logstash, Kibana) to receive and visualize API Manager analytics data
- [Datadog Analytics Installation Guide](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/monitoring/api-analytics/on-prem/datadog-installation-guide.md): Publish API Manager analytics events to Datadog for monitoring and alerting
- [OpenSearch Analytics Installation Guide](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/monitoring/api-analytics/on-prem/opensearch-installation-guide.md): Set up OpenSearch to receive and visualize API Manager analytics data
- [Choreo Analytics Architecture](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/monitoring/api-analytics/choreo-analytics/api-analytics-architecture.md): Architecture of the Choreo-based cloud analytics solution for WSO2 API Manager
- [Choreo Analytics Getting Started](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/monitoring/api-analytics/choreo-analytics/getting-started-guide.md): Connect WSO2 API Manager to the Choreo cloud analytics portal to view API usage dashboards
- [Choreo Analytics Role-based Access Control](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/monitoring/api-analytics/choreo-analytics/role-based-access-control.md): Configure role-based access control for the Choreo analytics dashboards
- [Choreo Analytics Alerts](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/monitoring/api-analytics/choreo-analytics/configure-alerts.md): Configure alert rules in Choreo analytics to get notified of anomalous API traffic patterns
- [Choreo Analytics via Proxy](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/monitoring/api-analytics/choreo-analytics/choreo-based-analytics-via-proxy.md): Route API Manager analytics events to Choreo through an HTTP proxy
- [Publish Analytics Events to External Systems](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/monitoring/api-analytics/samples/publishing-analytics-events-to-external-systems.md): Extend the analytics pipeline to publish API usage events to custom external systems
- [Publish Custom Analytics Event Data](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/monitoring/api-analytics/samples/publishing-custom-analytics-data.md): Add custom fields to API analytics events to capture business-specific metadata
- [Monetize an API](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/monitoring/api-monetization/monetizing-an-api.md): Enable usage-based API monetization by integrating Stripe as the billing engine and defining paid subscription tiers
- [Observability Overview](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/monitoring/observability/observability-overview.md): Overview of the three pillars of API Manager observability — logs, metrics, and distributed tracing
- [Configure Logs](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/monitoring/observability/configuring-logging.md): Configure log levels, appenders, and output formats for the API Manager runtime logs
- [Correlation Logs](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/monitoring/observability/monitoring-correlation-logs.md): Enable and use correlation logs to trace individual API requests across the Gateway and backend
- [HTTP Access Logs](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/monitoring/observability/monitoring-http-access-logs.md): Configure and interpret HTTP access logs for incoming API traffic at the Gateway
- [Audit Logs](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/monitoring/observability/monitoring-audit-logs.md): Monitor audit trails that capture administrative actions in the Publisher, Developer Portal, and Admin Portal
- [API Logs](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/monitoring/observability/monitoring-api-logs.md): Enable and view per-API request/response logs for debugging and compliance
- [WebSocket Logs](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/monitoring/observability/monitoring-websocket-logs.md): Monitor WebSocket connection and frame-level logs through the Classic Gateway
- [OpenSearch Log Monitoring](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/monitoring/observability/monitoring-with-opensearch.md): Ship API Manager logs to OpenSearch for centralized log aggregation and search
- [OpenTracing](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/monitoring/observability/traces/monitoring-with-opentracing.md): Enable OpenTracing in the Classic Gateway and export spans to Jaeger or Zipkin
- [OpenTelemetry](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/monitoring/observability/traces/monitoring-with-opentelemetry.md): Enable OpenTelemetry in the Classic Gateway and export traces to an OTLP-compatible backend
- [JMX-Based Monitoring](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/monitoring/observability/metrics/jmx-based-monitoring.md): Monitor API Manager runtime metrics (thread pools, connections, memory) via JMX using JConsole or similar tools

## Administration

- [Administration Overview](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/administer/admin-overview.md): Overview of all administrative tasks in WSO2 API Manager — user management, multitenancy, key managers, and rate limiting policies
- [Introduction to User Management](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/administer/managing-users-and-roles/introduction-to-user-management.md): Overview of user management in WSO2 API Manager — users, roles, and permissions hierarchy
- [Manage User Roles](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/administer/managing-users-and-roles/managing-user-roles.md): Create, modify, and delete user roles and assign permissions — covers the built-in admin, creator, publisher, and subscriber roles
- [Manage Users](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/administer/managing-users-and-roles/managing-users.md): Create, update, and delete user accounts in the API Manager user store
- [Manage Role Permissions](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/administer/managing-users-and-roles/managing-permissions.md): Configure the fine-grained permission tree to control what each role can access in the management console and portals
- [Manage Users for Admin Portal](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/administer/managing-users-and-roles/managing-users-for-admin-portal.md): Grant users access to the API Manager Admin Portal by assigning the admin role
- [Introduction to User Stores](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/administer/managing-users-and-roles/managing-user-stores/introduction-to-userstores.md): Overview of primary and secondary user store types — JDBC, LDAP, and Active Directory
- [Configure Secondary User Stores](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/administer/managing-users-and-roles/managing-user-stores/configuring-secondary-user-stores.md): Add secondary user stores to augment the primary user store for multi-directory user management
- [Write a Custom User Store Manager](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/administer/managing-users-and-roles/managing-user-stores/writing-a-custom-user-store-manager.md): Implement a custom Java-based user store manager to integrate with non-standard identity directories
- [Configure the Authorization Manager](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/administer/managing-users-and-roles/managing-user-stores/configuring-the-authorization-manager.md): Configure the authorization manager that handles role-permission mappings in the user store
- [Introduction to Multitenancy](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/administer/multitenancy/introduction-to-multitenancy.md): Overview of multitenancy in WSO2 API Manager — how tenants are isolated and share a single deployment
- [Manage Tenants](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/administer/multitenancy/managing-tenants.md): Create and manage tenant domains using the Management Console in a multi-tenant API Manager deployment
- [Configure Tenant Loading Policy](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/administer/multitenancy/configuring-the-tenant-loading-policy.md): Configure whether inactive tenants are loaded eagerly at startup or lazily on first access
- [Tenant Sharing with WSO2 IS 7.x](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/administer/multitenancy/tenant-sharing-with-wso2is7.md): Configure tenant domain sharing between WSO2 API Manager and WSO2 Identity Server 7.x
- [Monitor Server Health](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/administer/logging-and-monitoring/monitoring/monitoring-server-health.md): Check the health status of API Manager server nodes via the health check REST API endpoint
- [Configure a Gateway](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/administer/multiple-gateways/configure-gateway.md): Register and configure additional Gateway nodes in the Admin Portal for multi-gateway deployments
- [Configure Gateway Visibility](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/administer/multiple-gateways/configure-gateway-visibility.md): Control which tenants or API creators can see and deploy to each registered Gateway environment
- [Advanced Configurations](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/administer/advanced-configurations.md): Edit tenant-level advanced configuration settings in the Admin Portal's Settings > Advanced section using a JSON editor — includes options such as custom linter rules
- [Role-based Access Control for Admin Portal](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/administer/role-based-access-control.md): Restrict Admin Portal sections to specific admin roles for granular administrative access control
- [Manage Subscription Policies](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/administer/rate-limiting/manage-subscription-policies.md): Create and manage subscription-level business plan tiers (Bronze, Silver, Gold) that control request quotas per API subscription
- [Manage AI Subscription Policies](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/administer/rate-limiting/manage-ai-subscription-policies.md): Create token-based rate limiting tiers specifically for AI APIs to manage LLM usage and cost
- [Manage Application Policies](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/administer/rate-limiting/manage-application-policies.md): Create and manage application-level rate limiting tiers that cap total requests across all API subscriptions of an application
- [Manage Advanced Policies](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/administer/rate-limiting/manage-advanced-policies.md): Create advanced rate limiting policies with custom Siddhi conditions for IP, header, query parameter, or JWT claim-based throttling
- [Manage Deny Policies](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/administer/rate-limiting/manage-deny-policies.md): Create deny policies to block API traffic from specific IPs, users, or applications without modifying the API
- [Implement Custom Policies](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/administer/rate-limiting/implement-custom-policies.md): Write custom Siddhi applications to implement complex rate limiting logic beyond the built-in policy types
- [Change Default Tiers](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/administer/rate-limiting/change-default-tiers.md): Modify the quota and time-unit values of the default subscription and application rate limiting tiers
- [Governance Overview](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/administer/governance/overview.md): Overview of the governance capabilities in WSO2 API Manager — rulesets, compliance enforcement, and lifecycle governance for APIs and MCP servers
- [Governance Concepts](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/administer/governance/governance-concept.md): Core concepts of WSO2 API Manager governance — rulesets, policies, compliance states, and governance profiles
- [Governance Administrative Capabilities](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/administer/governance/api-governance-admin-capabilities.md): Admin Portal governance features — creating and managing rulesets, assigning governance policies, and viewing compliance dashboards
- [CI/CD-Driven Governance](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/administer/governance/api-governance-cicd.md): Integrate governance ruleset validation into CI/CD pipelines using the apictl governance CLI tool
- [Publisher Portal in Read-Only Mode](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/administer/publisher-portal-in-read-only-mode.md): Configure the Publisher portal to be read-only so API creators can view but not modify APIs

## APIOps

- [CI/CD for APIs Overview](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/apiops/cli/ci-cd-with-wso2-api-management.md): Overview of API CI/CD with WSO2 API Manager — how apictl automates API promotion across environments in a pipeline
- [Build a CI/CD Pipeline Using the CLI](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/apiops/cli/cicd-using-cli.md): Step-by-step guide to building a CI/CD pipeline for APIs using the apictl command-line tool
- [Build a CI/CD Pipeline Using Jenkins](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/apiops/cli/building-jenkins-ci-cd-pipeline.md): Integrate apictl with Jenkins to automate API deployment across environments in a Jenkins pipeline
- [Getting Started with apictl](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/apiops/cli/getting-started-with-wso2-api-controller.md): Download and initialize the WSO2 API Controller (apictl) CLI tool and connect it to an API Manager environment
- [API Governance CLI Tool](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/apiops/cli/api-governance-cli-tool.md): Use the apictl governance commands to validate API definitions against governance rulesets as part of a CI/CD pipeline
- [Manage APIs and API Products](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/apiops/cli/managing-apis-api-products/managing-apis-and-api-products.md): Use apictl to list, get, delete, and change the lifecycle state of APIs and API Products across environments
- [Import APIs via Dev First Approach](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/apiops/cli/managing-apis-api-products/importing-apis-via-dev-first-approach.md): Create an API project from an OpenAPI spec using apictl and import it to an API Manager environment without using the Publisher portal
- [Migrate APIs to Different Environments](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/apiops/cli/managing-apis-api-products/migrating-apis-to-different-environments.md): Export APIs from one API Manager environment and import them to another using apictl for environment promotion
- [Migrate API Products to Different Environments](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/apiops/cli/managing-apis-api-products/migrating-api-products-to-different-environments.md): Export and import API Products (with or without their dependent APIs) across API Manager environments using apictl
- [Manage MCP Servers](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/apiops/cli/managing-mcp-servers/managing-mcp-servers.md): Use apictl to list, get, and manage MCP servers across API Manager environments
- [Import MCP Servers via Dev First Approach](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/apiops/cli/managing-mcp-servers/importing-mcp-servers-via-dev-first-approach.md): Create an MCP server project and import it to an API Manager environment using apictl without the Publisher portal
- [Migrate MCP Servers to Different Environments](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/apiops/cli/managing-mcp-servers/migrating-mcp-servers-to-different-environments.md): Export and import MCP servers across API Manager environments using apictl
- [Manage Applications](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/apiops/cli/managing-applications/managing-applications.md): Use apictl to list and manage Developer Portal applications across environments
- [Migrate Applications to Different Environments](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/apiops/cli/managing-applications/migrating-applications-to-different-environments.md): Export Developer Portal applications from one environment and import them to another using apictl
- [Manage Rate Limiting Policies](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/apiops/cli/managing-rate-limiting-policies/throttle-policy-import-export.md): Use apictl to import and export subscription, application, and advanced rate limiting policies
- [Migrate Rate Limiting Policies to Different Environments](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/apiops/cli/managing-rate-limiting-policies/migrating-rate-limiting-policies-to-different-environments.md): Promote rate limiting policies from one API Manager environment to another using apictl
- [Manage Common API Policies](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/apiops/cli/managing-common-api-policies/managing-common-api-policies.md): Use apictl to import and export reusable API policies across API Manager environments
- [Migrate Common API Policies to Different Environments](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/apiops/cli/managing-common-api-policies/migrating-common-api-policies-to-different-environments.md): Export common API policies from one environment and import them to another using apictl
- [Encrypt Secrets with apictl](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/apiops/cli/encrypting-secrets-with-ctl.md): Encrypt sensitive configuration values (passwords, tokens) in API project files using apictl before committing to version control
- [Enable Correlation Logs with apictl](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/apiops/cli/enabling-correlation-logs-with-apictl.md): Toggle correlation logging on API Manager environments programmatically using apictl
- [AI-Related Operations with apictl](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/apiops/cli/ai-related-operations-with-apictl.md): Use apictl commands to manage AI service providers, AI subscription policies, and AI APIs
- [Create Custom Users for apictl Operations](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/apiops/cli/advanced-topics/creating-custom-users-to-perform-api-controller-operations.md): Create minimally privileged users with only the permissions needed for apictl CI/CD operations
- [Configure Environment-Specific Parameters](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/apiops/cli/advanced-topics/configuring-environment-specific-parameters.md): Use parameter files to override API configuration values (endpoints, credentials) per target environment during import
- [Use Dynamic Data in apictl Projects](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/apiops/cli/advanced-topics/using-dynamic-data-in-api-controller-projects.md): Inject environment variables and dynamic values into API project configuration files at import time
- [Configure Different Endpoint Types](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/apiops/cli/advanced-topics/configuring-different-endpoint-types.md): Configure HTTP, load-balanced, failover, and AWS Lambda endpoints in apictl API project files
- [Configure Endpoint Security Types](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/apiops/cli/advanced-topics/configuring-different-endpoint-security-types.md): Configure Basic Auth, Digest Auth, and OAuth2 endpoint security in apictl API project parameter files
- [Format the Output of Get Commands](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/apiops/cli/advanced-topics/formatting-the-output-of-get-command.md): Customize the output format of apictl list commands using Go template format strings
- [Configure Git Integration](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/apiops/cli/advanced-topics/configuring-git-integration.md): Set up apictl Git integration to automatically detect and deploy changed API projects from a Git repository

## Reference

- [Product REST APIs Overview](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/reference/product-apis/overview.md): Index of all REST APIs exposed by API Manager — Publisher, Developer Portal, Admin, Gateway, Service Catalog, DevOps, and Governance APIs
- [Publisher API v4](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/reference/product-apis/publisher-apis/publisher-v4/publisher-v4.md): API reference for the Publisher REST API v4 — create, update, and manage APIs programmatically
- [Developer Portal API v3](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/reference/product-apis/devportal-apis/devportal-v3/devportal-v3.md): API reference for the Developer Portal REST API v3 — manage applications, subscriptions, and keys programmatically
- [Admin API v4](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/reference/product-apis/admin-apis/admin-v4/admin-v4.md): API reference for the Admin REST API v4 — manage rate limiting policies, key managers, and workflows programmatically
- [Gateway API v2](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/reference/product-apis/gateway-apis/gateway-v2/gateway-v2.md): API reference for the Gateway REST API v2 — manage API artifacts on the Classic Gateway runtime
- [Service Catalog API v1](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/reference/product-apis/service-catalog-apis/service-catalog-v1/service-catalog-v1.md): API reference for the Service Catalog REST API v1 — register and manage integration services for API creation
- [DevOps API v0](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/reference/product-apis/devops-apis/devops-v0/devops-v0.md): API reference for the DevOps REST API — manage application or API specific runtime logs and configurations
- [Governance API v1](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/reference/product-apis/governance-apis/governance-v1/governance-v1.md): API reference for the Governance REST API v1 — manage rulesets, governance policies, and compliance data programmatically
- [Product APIs Advanced Configurations](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/reference/product-apis/advanced-configurations.md): Advanced configuration options for the API Manager REST API layer
- [Understanding the New Configuration Model](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/reference/understanding-the-new-configuration-model.md): Overview of the TOML-based configuration model introduced in API Manager 3.0 — all settings consolidated in deployment.toml
- [API-M Config Catalog](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/reference/config-catalog.md): Complete reference of all deployment.toml configuration parameters for WSO2 API Manager
- [API Controller (APICTL) Reference](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/reference/apictl/wso2-api-controller.md): Full command reference for the WSO2 API Controller (apictl) CLI tool
- [Rule Validator](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/reference/governance/rule-validator/rule-validator.md): Technical reference for the WSO2 Rule Validator — a Java implementation of Spectral used to lint API definitions against governance rulesets
- [WSO2 API Management Guidelines Ruleset](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/reference/governance/wso2-api-management-guidelines.md): Built-in governance ruleset enforcing WSO2's API management best practices — naming, versioning, and metadata requirements
- [WSO2 REST API Design Guidelines Ruleset](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/reference/governance/wso2-rest-api-design-guidelines.md): Built-in governance ruleset enforcing WSO2's REST API design standards — resource naming, HTTP methods, and response codes
- [OWASP Top 10 Ruleset](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/reference/governance/owasp-top-10.md): Built-in governance ruleset that checks API definitions against OWASP API Security Top 10 vulnerabilities
- [API YAML Representation](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/reference/governance/api-yaml-representation.md): Reference for the YAML format used to represent API metadata in apictl project files and governance policies
- [API Documentation YAML Representation](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/reference/governance/api-doc-yaml-representation.md): Reference for the YAML format used to represent API documentation in apictl project files
- [Vendor Specific Extensions](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/reference/vendor-extensions-catalog.md): Reference for WSO2-specific OpenAPI extensions (x-auth-type, x-throttling-tier, x-wso2-cors, etc.) used in API definitions
- [Extend Key Validation](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/reference/customize-product/extending-api-manager/extending-key-management/extending-key-validation.md): Implement a custom key validation handler to add extra validation logic during token validation at the Gateway
- [Extend Scope Validation](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/reference/customize-product/extending-api-manager/extending-key-management/extending-scope-validation.md): Implement a custom scope validator to apply business-specific authorization logic beyond role-based scope matching
- [Extend the Key Manager Interface](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/reference/customize-product/extending-api-manager/extending-key-management/extending-the-key-manager-interface.md): Implement a custom Key Manager connector by extending the KeyManager Java interface
- [Write Custom Grant Types](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/reference/customize-product/extending-api-manager/extending-key-management/writing-custom-grant-types.md): Implement a custom OAuth2 grant type handler for non-standard token issuance flows
- [Customize API Template for Gateway](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/reference/customize-product/extending-api-manager/extending-gateway/customizing-api-template-for-gateway.md): Override the default Synapse API template used when deploying APIs to the Classic Gateway
- [Write Custom Handlers](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/reference/customize-product/extending-api-manager/extending-gateway/writing-custom-handlers.md): Implement a custom Gateway handler (Java) to add processing logic to the Classic Gateway request/response pipeline
- [Invoke the API Manager from the BPEL Engine](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/reference/customize-product/extending-api-manager/extending-workflows/invoking-the-api-manager-from-the-bpel-engine.md): Integrate a BPEL engine with API Manager workflow callbacks for long-running approval processes
- [Customize a Workflow Extension](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/reference/customize-product/extending-api-manager/extending-workflows/customizing-a-workflow-extension.md): Implement a custom workflow executor to control approval logic for API Manager lifecycle events
- [Configure HTTP Redirection for Workflows](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/reference/customize-product/extending-api-manager/extending-workflows/configuring-http-redirection-for-workflows.md): Configure HTTP callbacks for workflow approval systems that use redirect-based response handling
- [Configure Workflows for Tenants](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/reference/customize-product/extending-api-manager/extending-workflows/configuring-workflows-for-tenants.md): Configure separate workflow executors for individual tenants in a multi-tenant deployment
- [Configure Workflows in a Cluster](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/reference/customize-product/extending-api-manager/extending-workflows/configuring-workflows-in-a-cluster.md): Set up workflow approval callbacks to work correctly in a clustered API Manager deployment
- [Change the Default User Role in Workflows](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/reference/customize-product/extending-api-manager/extending-workflows/changing-the-default-user-role-in-workflows.md): Change the user role that receives and approves workflow tasks from the default admin role to a custom role
- [Clean Up Workflow Tasks](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/reference/customize-product/extending-api-manager/extending-workflows/cleaning-up-workflow-tasks.md): Remove stale or orphaned workflow task entries from the API Manager database
- [Configure SSO with SAML2](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/reference/customize-product/extending-api-manager/saml2-sso/configuring-single-sign-on-with-saml2.md): Configure SAML2-based single sign-on for the Publisher and Developer Portal web applications
- [Configure External IDP for SSO via IS](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/reference/customize-product/extending-api-manager/saml2-sso/configuring-external-idp-through-identity-server-for-sso.md): Use WSO2 Identity Server to federate SSO to an external identity provider for API Manager portals
- [Configure IS as IDP for SSO](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/reference/customize-product/extending-api-manager/saml2-sso/configuring-identity-server-as-idp-for-sso.md): Configure WSO2 Identity Server as the SAML2 identity provider for API Manager portal SSO
- [Multi-Factor Authentication for Portals](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/reference/customize-product/extending-api-manager/saml2-sso/multi-factor-authentication-mfa-for-publisher-and-developer-portals.md): Enable multi-factor authentication for the Publisher and Developer Portal login flows
- [Override the Developer Portal Theme](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/reference/customize-product/customizations/customizing-the-developer-portal/overriding-developer-portal-theme.md): Customize the visual theme of the Developer Portal by overriding default CSS and component styles
- [API Category-Based Grouping](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/reference/customize-product/customizations/customizing-the-developer-portal/customize-api-listing/api-category-based-grouping.md): Organize Developer Portal API listings using custom categories instead of tags
- [Change the Default View of API Listing](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/reference/customize-product/customizations/customizing-the-developer-portal/customize-api-listing/change-default-view.md): Change the default API listing view in the Developer Portal from grid to list or vice versa
- [Enable or Disable API Detail Tabs](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/reference/customize-product/customizations/customizing-the-developer-portal/enabling-or-disabling-api-detail-tabs.md): Show or hide individual tabs (Overview, Documentation, Try Out, etc.) on the Developer Portal API detail page
- [Override API Overview Page per API](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/reference/customize-product/customizations/customizing-the-developer-portal/override-api-overview-page-per-api.md): Provide a custom Markdown overview for individual APIs in the Developer Portal by editing the API description in the Publisher portal
- [Enable or Disable Rating](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/reference/customize-product/customizations/customizing-the-developer-portal/enable-or-disable-rating.md): Toggle the API star rating feature on or off in the Developer Portal
- [Enable or Disable Home Page](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/reference/customize-product/customizations/customizing-the-developer-portal/enable-or-disable-home-page.md): Show or hide the Developer Portal home page with featured APIs and banners
- [Enable or Disable Tag Cloud](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/reference/customize-product/customizations/customizing-the-developer-portal/enable-or-disable-tag-cloud.md): Toggle the API tag cloud widget on the Developer Portal
- [Enable or Disable Footer](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/reference/customize-product/customizations/customizing-the-developer-portal/enable-or-disable-footer.md): Show or hide the footer section of the Developer Portal
- [Enable or Disable Banner](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/reference/customize-product/customizations/customizing-the-developer-portal/enable-or-disable-banner.md): Show or hide the banner section at the top of the Developer Portal
- [Styling API Details Left Menu](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/reference/customize-product/customizations/customizing-the-developer-portal/styling-api-details-left-menu.md): Customize the left-side navigation menu on the API detail page of the Developer Portal
- [Styling the Logo and Header](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/reference/customize-product/customizations/customizing-the-developer-portal/styling-the-logo-and-header.md): Replace the default logo and customize the header color and layout of the Developer Portal
- [Enable or Disable Self Signup](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/reference/customize-product/customizations/customizing-the-developer-portal/enabling-or-disabling-self-signup.md): Allow or prevent users from self-registering new accounts on the Developer Portal
- [Configure reCaptcha for Self-SignUp](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/reference/customize-product/customizations/customizing-the-developer-portal/configuring-recaptcha-for-self-signup.md): Add Google reCaptcha to the Developer Portal self-registration form to prevent bot signups
- [Override the Publisher Portal Theme](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/reference/customize-product/customizations/overriding-the-publisher-portal-theme.md): Customize the visual theme of the Publisher portal by overriding default styles and branding
- [Log in Using Social Media](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/reference/customize-product/customizations/log-in-to-the-dev-portal-using-social-media.md): Configure social login (Google, Facebook, etc.) for the Developer Portal using federated authentication
- [Direct the Root Context to the Developer Portal](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/reference/customize-product/customizations/directing-the-root-context-to-the-developer-portal.md): Redirect the root URL of the API Manager host to the Developer Portal instead of the default Carbon Management Console
- [Customize User Signup in Developer Portal](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/reference/customize-product/customizations/customizing-user-signup-in-developer-portal.md): Add custom fields and validation logic to the Developer Portal user self-registration form
- [Customize Login Pages](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/reference/customize-product/customizations/customizing-login-pages-for-dev-portal-and-publisher.md): Customize the login page HTML and styling for the Developer Portal and Publisher portal
- [Customize Gateway and Store URLs for Tenants](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/reference/customize-product/customizations/customize-the-api-store-and-gateway-urls-for-tenants.md): Configure custom domain URLs for the Developer Portal and Gateway per tenant
- [Add a User Signup Workflow](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/reference/customize-product/customizations/adding-a-user-signup-workflow.md): Add an admin-approval workflow to the Developer Portal user self-registration process
- [Add Internationalization](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/reference/customize-product/customizations/adding-internationalization.md): Add support for additional languages in the Developer Portal using i18n resource bundles
- [Define Custom Linter Rules](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/reference/customize-product/customizations/define-custom-linters.md): Create and register custom Spectral linting rulesets to enforce organization-specific API design standards
- [Advanced UI Customization](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/reference/customize-product/customizations/advanced-ui-customization.md): Perform deep UI customizations to the Developer Portal and Publisher portal by overriding React components
- [Modify Workflow Approval Task Limit](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/reference/customize-product/customizations/modify-workflow-approval-task-limit.md): Change the maximum number of pending workflow approval tasks displayed in the Admin Portal
- [Implementing a Custom Validation Engine](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/reference/customize-product/customizations/implementing-a-custom-validation-engine.md): Replace the default OpenAPI validation engine with a custom implementation for specialized schema validation requirements
- [Admin Services](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/reference/wso2-admin-services.md): Reference for WSO2 Admin Services — the underlying SOAP-based management services accessible via the Management Console
- [Work with the Source Code](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/reference/working-with-the-source-code.md): Guide to building WSO2 API Manager from source code
- [Java Documentation](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/reference/java-documentation.md): Links to the Javadoc API documentation for WSO2 API Manager extension points and interfaces
- [WSO2 API Manager Best Practices](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/reference/wso2-api-manager-best-practices.md): Recommended guidelines for API naming, versioning, resource design, and endpoint configuration
- [Best Practices for Working with Endpoints](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/reference/best-practices-endpoints.md): Recommendations for configuring reliable, secure, and high-performance endpoint connections in the API Gateway
- [Accessibility Compliance](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/reference/accessibility-compliance.md): WSO2 API Manager accessibility compliance statement for the Publisher and Developer Portal web interfaces
- [Message Flow in the API Manager Gateway](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/reference/guides/message-flow-in-the-api-manager-gateway.md): Detailed walkthrough of how a request flows through the Classic Gateway — handlers, mediators, and backend invocation
- [Accessing API Manager by Multiple Devices](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/reference/guides/accessing-api-manager-by-multiple-devices-simultaneously.md): Configuration guidance for supporting concurrent access to the API Manager portals from multiple client devices
- [Directory Structure of WSO2 Products](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/reference/guides/directory-structure-of-wso2-products.md): Reference for the directory layout of the WSO2 API Manager installation package
- [Common Runtime and Configuration Artifacts](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/reference/common-runtime-and-configuration-artifacts.md): Reference for common configuration files and runtime artifacts shared across WSO2 products
- [Default Product Ports](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/reference/default-product-ports.md): List of all default port numbers used by API Manager components — HTTP, HTTPS, and management ports
- [Product Compatibility](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/reference/product-compatibility.md): Compatibility details of the WSO2 API Manager 4.7.0 runtimes
- [API Manager Performance Test Results](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/reference/performance-tests-results.md): Benchmark test results for WSO2 API Manager throughput and latency under various load conditions
- [Token Persistence Performance Test Results](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/reference/performance-test-results-token-persistence.md): Performance test results comparing token persistence modes (enabled vs. disabled) in WSO2 API Manager
- [Supported Cipher Suites](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/reference/supported-cipher-suites.md): List of TLS cipher suites supported by WSO2 API Manager for HTTPS transport configuration
- [Error Handling](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/reference/troubleshooting/error-handling.md): Reference for default Gateway error responses and how to customize fault sequences for different error types
- [Capturing System Data in Error Situations](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/reference/troubleshooting/capturing-system-data-in-error-situations.md): How to capture thread dumps, heap dumps, and log bundles to diagnose API Manager failures
- [Troubleshooting in Production Environments](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/reference/troubleshooting/admin-troubleshooting-in-production-environments.md): Common production issues and diagnostic approaches for WSO2 API Manager
- [Utilizing Runtime Diagnostic Tool](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/reference/troubleshooting/utilizing-runtime-diagnostic-tool.md): Use the Runtime Diagnostic Tool to automatically collect diagnostic data (thread dumps, logs) during incidents
- [Cleaning Up Partially Created Keys](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/reference/troubleshooting/cleaning-up-partially-created-keys.md): Remove orphaned OAuth2 consumer key/secret pairs left behind by failed key generation attempts
- [Configure XSLT Mediation with Xalan](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/reference/troubleshooting/configuring-xslt-mediation-with-xalan.md): Use the Xalan XSLT processor instead of the default JDK processor for XML payload transformation in the Gateway
- [Troubleshooting Invalid Callback Error](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/reference/troubleshooting/troubleshooting-invalid-callback-error.md): Diagnose and fix the "Registered callback does not match with the provided url" OAuth2 error
- [Troubleshooting JMS](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/reference/troubleshooting/troubleshooting-jms.md): Common JMS transport issues in WSO2 API Manager and their solutions
- [Troubleshooting WebSocket APIs](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/reference/troubleshooting/troubleshooting-websocket-api.md): Diagnose and resolve common WebSocket API issues in the Classic Gateway
- [FAQ](https://apim.docs.wso2.com/en/4.7.0/reference/faq.md): Frequently asked questions about WSO2 API Manager — product capabilities, licensing, deployment, and configuration
